releases.name: Winlator Mali Bionic 1.1
releases.body:
Winlator Mali Bionic v1.1

🚀 New Features

- Added Fake HDR.
- Added a Universal FPS Limiter.
- Added a Sharpness option.
- Added GTA V Optimization toggle in Shortcut Settings.
- Added DXVK Config toggle with built-in pre-made configuration presets.
- Automatically generates a dxvk.conf file for each container based on the selected preset.
- Added new DXVK versions.
- Added the new GameNative Vulkan Wrapper.
- Added new Box64 and FEX presets.

---

🎮 Unreal Engine 3 Preset

- Added a dedicated Unreal Engine 3 preset.
- Tested with X-Men Origins: Wolverine for improved compatibility and performance.

---

⚙️ Hybrid x86 FEXCore

- Added a custom Hybrid x86 FEXCore.

What does it do?

- Improves compatibility while maintaining high performance.
- Fixes physics issues in supported games that normally require x86 Reduced Precision to be completely disabled.

Why is it better?

Normally, disabling x86 Reduced Precision forces software emulation for all x86 code, which can significantly reduce performance.

Hybrid x86 only uses software emulation where it is actually needed (such as physics calculations), while allowing the rest of the game to run natively for much better performance.

Tested Games

- ✅ Assassin's Creed Revelations – Physics issues fixed while using the Performance preset.
- ✅ Assassin's Creed Brotherhood – Physics issues fixed while using the Performance preset.
- ⚠️ Assassin's Creed III – Still requires x86 Reduced Precision to be fully disabled.

«Note: Hybrid x86 improves compatibility for many games but is not a universal solution.»

---

⚡ Improvements

- Improved ImageFS and Wine installation speeds by replacing TZST packages, resulting in significantly faster extraction and setup times.
- Updated various DXVK, Box64, FEX, Vulkan Wrappers, and compatibility presets.
- Improved overall compatibility, stability, and performance across supported games.

---

🛠️ Fixes

- Fixed an issue where Community Configs could fail to import correctly.
- Fixed an issue that could cause normal containers to become corrupted.
- Fixed a crash that occurred when selecting certain VKD3D versions.
- Fixed an issue where enabling APEX Frame Generation could randomly produce visual artifacts during gameplay.
- Fixed an issue where enabling Screen Effects could cause the image to become stretched.
- Fixed an issue where memory was not being released properly after closing the app, causing RAM usage to remain higher than expected.

---

⚠️ Important

- A clean reinstall is required for this release. Existing installations are not supported due to changes in the system files and package format.

---

📝 Additional Changes

- Added numerous other fixes, improvements, optimizations, and internal changes that are not individually listed in this changelog.
- Updated various internal components to improve the overall user experience.
- More game-specific presets, compatibility improvements, and optimizations will continue to be added in future releases.

---

❤️ Special Thanks

- Winlator Bionic — Pipetto-crypto
- Winlator Ludashi — StevenMxz
- Controller Fixes — Vivsi1
- leegao
- BannerHub — the412banner

---

📞 Contact / Support

If you need help, want to report bugs, or would like to support the project:

- Telegram Group: https://t.me/+RDjo9_ORqXgwODc1
- Ko-fi: https://ko-fi.com/gunacharanteja
- UPI (India): gunacharanteja@ybl

Note : Ludashi Apk Has Been Updated Again Solving Incorrect Package Name.

releases.name: Winlator Mali Bionic 1.0 ( Updated Again )
releases.body:
Winlator Mali Bionic v1.0

New Features

- Multiple Vulkan Wrapper Selection — Select between Wrapper, Wrapper-Leegao, and Wrapper V2 on a per-container basis.
- ASTC Transcoding — Native ASTC texture transcoding using a modified version of leegao's bcn_layer with improved performance, memory usage, and stability.
- ETC2 Transcoding — Native ETC2 texture transcoding using a modified version of leegao's bcn_layer with improved performance, memory usage, and stability.
- Apex Frame Generation — Based on Motion Compute Frame Interpolator (MCFI), with additional implementation and optimizations for improved performance, stability, and compatibility. Apex also utilizes GPU resources alongside the game and may impact performance depending on your device.
- Advanced HUD — Enhanced in-game overlay based on Winlator Ludashi's HUD with additional refinements.
- Unified Control System — XInput, DInput, and Exclusive input modes have been unified into a single control system.
- Graphics Driver Manager — Install and manage Vulkan Wrappers and bcn_layer directly from local storage. User-installed drivers automatically take priority over bundled versions.
- Gyro View — Control the in-game camera using your device's gyroscope.
- Revamped Controls UI — Redesigned control icons with a cleaner and more consistent interface.
- Integrated File Manager — File Manager integrated from Winlator Ludashi.
- Improved Shortcut Cards — Redesigned game shortcuts with cover art support.
- Cover Art Manager — Built-in cover art management for game shortcuts.
- Automatic Game Detection — Automatically detects game names from their default executables via Steam.
- Automatic Cover Art Fetching — Automatically downloads cover art from SteamGridDB.
- Community Configs — Native implementation inspired by the original concept by the412banner / Bannerhub.
- Proton 10 ARM64EC by Default — Proton 10 ARM64EC is now the default Wine runtime.
- Wine Component Overrides — Configure DirectInput ("dinput", "dinput8") and "bulletin" to Native directly from Container Settings.
- And More — Additional improvements and optimizations throughout the application.

Notes

- Tested on MediaTek Dimensity 7200.
- The built-in ASTC and ETC2 transcoding modes use a modified version of leegao's original implementation. If you prefer the original version, download it from this release page and import it using Manage Graphics Drivers from the main menu.
- Source Is Released And Updated .

Special Thanks

- Winlator Bionic — Pipetto-crypto
- Winlator Ludashi — StevenMxz
- Vivsi1-Controller Fixes
- leegao
- the412banner / Bannerhub

Contact / Support Me : 

Telegram Group : https://t.me/+RDjo9_ORqXgwODc1

Ko-fi : https://ko-fi.com/gunacharanteja

The Apks Are Updated Again With Minor Bug Fixes And Improvements Please Update ( Recommend Re-install ).

# Added

• Latest Box64 Is Added In Proot Version

• Added Cube D3D & SphereMap 3D Test 

• Added D3D9 Wrapper By ThirteenAG In Start Menu → Install →  WineD3D → Experimental 

• Added Core Fonts

• Added Additional x86 and X64 Visual C Packages

• Added Xna Framework 

• Added An Option To Backup And Import Registry And Saves Of Container 

• Many Other Things Are Also Added 

# New Game Presets 

• Fixed And Added Back Skyrim Game Preset 

• Added Virtua Tennis 3 & 4 Game Preset 

• Added Silent Hill Home Coming Game Preset 

# Changes

• Wine 9.2 Is Added And Made Default In Glibc 

• Game UX Is Disabled By Default ( This Fixes Virtua Tennis 4 And More ) 

• The Fixes Section Has Been Updated 

• Some Games Presets Are Updated 

• Many Other Things Are Changed And Updated 

# Note 

• I Decided To Only Upload Proot_Antutu And Glibc From Now

• Delete The Old Apk And Install The New One 

• Proot Is The Recommended Version 

• Glibc Apk Is Updated Again On Dec 1 2024 . Pls Update

Changes : 

•  Proot Builds Are Now Based On Winlator 8.0 Finally More Compatibility 

• 2.1 Is As Set As The Default GL VERSION

• Disable Vertex Array BGRA Is Disabled By Default 

• Default Gpu Model In Proot Builds Is Now Set To Nvdia GeForce 9800GT So Oblivion Launcher And More Can Detect And Give Settings 

• Box64 Version 0.2.8 From Winlator 7.1 Is Now The Default Version

Added :

• WineD3D 9.20 Added 

• BOX64_MMAP32 Environment Variable Added For Proot Builds

• More Things Are Added Which I Can't Remember Now Sorry 

Updated : 

• Updated The 3D Test ( Use This Instead Cube D3D Test It Is Removed Now )

• Updated VC Redist ( Improves Stability If Installed )

• Prince Of Persia 2008 And Prince Of Persia The Forgotten Sands Game Presets Are Added 

Removed :

Note : 

• To Fix Audio Issue On PES Games Just Turn Off Wine Esync In Container Settings 

• Skyrim Preset Is Now Removed From Proot Build Because Winlator 8.0 Doesn't Support To Disable Wow64 ( You Can Still Play Skyrim In WinlatorMali 1.8 And 1.7 )

releases.name: Horizon V4 - Not Connected fix
releases.body: After minor changes like Box64, Wine (all have almost the same performance), DXVK and Turnip and me not having time to do anything with this project, it's finally getting a "Not Connected" fix related to the usage of /tmp directory on some devices by the termux-x11 library in the app. Should work on Android 15 with this fix as well, people already reported it working

releases.name: Turnip A8XX v29
releases.body:
A8XX Turnip Vulkan V29
Built from: https://github.com/whitebelyash/mesa-unified (turnip/gen8 branch).


>[!WARNING]
> Please don't report issues to Mesa, PLEASE
          
Supported GPUs:
* Adreno 8XX Family: 840, 830, 829, 825, 810
* Adreno 7XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip + 710/720 from Termux patches (unstable)
* Adreno 6XX Family: any GPU supported by the upstream Turnip

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!  
Adreno 810 is additionally very slow, I'm not sure if you even want to use Turnip there

Notes:
* Disable DXVK_HUD if you see weird black artifacts
* Apparently there are swizzling glitches in Eden & maybe other emulators, try increasing render scale(?)
* Some games may require AMD/NVIDIA GPU to run and refuse to start on Qualcomm or block some features. I've included a patch to spoof as Steam Deck - use `TU_DEBUG=deck_emu`
* GMEM rendering produces glitchy picture on A830 (write page faults), disable it with `TU_DEBUG=sysmem`. It's pretty much useless for PC emulation anyway.

Changes:
* Rebase onto upstream
* Some PRAGMATA fixes.

GPUs other than 830/840 can have reduced performance and/or more glitches. Can't do anything about that, sorry!

Changes:

* tu, freedreno: Fix CP_CCHE_INVALIDATE not being applied at the right point 
* ir3/shared_ra: fix live-out reload after src reload

* turnip: Fix D16 depth clear rounding mismatch in sysmem mode 
* ir3/ra: fix killed src detection while spilling

releases.name: Winlator 10.2 (Revision 2 Beta) Glibc unstable - Early Access
releases.body:
[![logo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bd81f1a1-2391-4c39-abbd-a42b1f5ef65f)](https://github.com/winebox64/winlator/releases)

# Released for personal use ### Winlator 10.2 (beta) - Now smaller, lighter, and faster! Save storage with optimized app packaged in 130MB and installed in just 1.3GB. - Updated latest DXVK Async (v2.6.1), and **DXVK-II ULL (v2.5.3.94)** focus on ultra low latency ~8ms at 60 FPS, ~4ms at 120 FPS, with slightly lower fps (small performance impact), Improves input responsiveness on NFS: Most Wanted 2012. ![lv_0_20250323083625](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a0aab4ec-0637-4cd2-a171-8ad5a76d91cb) **Definition:** *What is ULL? Ultra-low latency refers to an extremely short delay or lag between a user's input and the system's response. It's measured in milliseconds (ms) or even nanoseconds (ns).* | *Input Latency* | *Gaming Experience* | *Suitability* | | --- | --- | --- | | 0-30 ms | Excellent | Competitive Gaming | | 30-50 ms | Good | Casual Gaming | | 50-100 ms | Fair | Non-Competitive Play | | 100-200 ms | Poor | Not Recommended | | 200+ ms | Unacceptable | Unplayable | - Updated DXVK HUD scale and opacity options have been added. - Updated 2 new configuration presets are available: `optmx11` for improved graphics and `perfx11` for better performance, the choice is yours. - ZINK_DEBUG has been updated with additional options - Zink Descriptors has been updated with additional options. - Updated components now fixed. - The loading is now way smoother and loads faster! - Updated Screen size resolutions and wider aspect ratio. - Updated FrameRate options - Updated environment variables are now activated by default. ### Winlator 10.0 (beta) - Added missing MPEG-1 gstreamer plugin [#269](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator/issues/269) - Added workaround for Dino Crisis 1/2 video issues - Updated Box64 (v0.3.4 release).~ - Added native GLIBC for better I/O performance - Minor improvements to Vortek - Improved internal Registry Editor (Speeds up container startup) - Added option to open Android browser from Wine - Added option to use Android clipboard on Wine - Added option to capture pointer on external mouse - Added some network stuff on [Wine](https://github.com/brunodev85/wine-9.2-custom/commit/3437f8ee1617ba97550c96fb2dc4e210420aae93) (Fixes some games startup) - Implemented direct rendering on XServer and [Vulkan WSI X11](https://github.com/brunodev85/mesa3d-custom/blob/main/turnip-25.0.0/src/vulkan/wsi/wsi_common_x11.c) (Turnip). ![animation](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/937e1f8a-2b21-4e26-95a7-37020400bc60) ---- ### ⚠️ **Results can vary based on the device used, environmental conditions, and other factors, such as simultaneous processing, and different processor capabilities.** **If you experience errors or crashes, try changing DXVK, Graphics driver or Box64.*

releases.name: Minimum System Requirements for Winlator WB64
releases.body:
[![logo](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ed1525e8-b05f-4e93-8003-933146b27dd9)](https://github.com/winebox64/winlator/releases)



# Minimum System Requirements for Winlator WB64:
- __OS (Operating System):__ Android 7.0 or later
- __Processor/CPU (Central Processing Unit):__ Qualcomm Snapdragon 460 or higher
- __GPU (Graphics Processing Unit):__ Adreno 610 or higher 
- __Graphics API:__ Vulkan 1.1 or higher
- __Internal Storage:__ At least 2GB of free space
- __RAM (Random Access Memory):__ At least 1GB of free RAM available, depending on the game requirments
- __Root Access:__ Not required
- __Gamepad:__ Optional (xinput/xbox recommended)


__NOTE: Recommended for game titles released before 2013 or DX9-based, Some games especially those from 2013 onwards, may experience performance issues.__
__Results can vary based on the device used, environmental conditions, and other factors, such as simultaneous processing, and different processor capabilities.__

__Please be careful when downloading from other websites. We recommend downloading only from this GitHub repository or official.__

✅ __[WINLATOR.ORG](https://winlator.org) is the original site.__ 

⚠️ __while [~WINLATOR.COM~](https://github.com/winebox64/winlator/releases), [~WINLATOR.APP~](https://github.com/winebox64/winlator/releases) and others are fake.__
__Never share your E-mail address or any other personal information.__ ⚠️

----

### Why does glibc run slower than Proot on some Android devices?
**1. glibc vs. Android’s bionic libc**
Android uses bionic libc, which is lightweight and optimized for mobile devices.
Running glibc on Android often requires additional compatibility layers, like proot + glibc rootfs, which might introduce performance overhead.
**2. Syscall Translation Overhead**
Proot uses syscall translation, which can be slow in general.
However, on Android, some glibc syscalls may not be well-optimized or may rely on emulation layers, making native glibc performance worse.
**3. Filesystem Overhead**
Proot uses overlay filesystems, which can sometimes optimize game data access.
A direct glibc setup might suffer from filesystem incompatibilities on Android.
**4. Memory & CPU Constraints**
Android optimizes bionic for low-memory usage.
Running glibc-based games might trigger higher RAM usage, leading to CPU throttling or inefficient memory access.
**5. Native vs. Emulated Execution**
Proot can sometimes optimize syscall handling by redirecting them efficiently.
glibc-based games might not be fully optimized for Android’s kernel, leading to bottlenecks.

releases.name: Bannerhub v3.8.0
releases.body: > [!WARNING] > **Only install official stable releases** (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or raw CI artifacts — pre-release builds use a different package name (`com.tencent.ig`) and cannot be upgraded to stable without uninstalling first. > [!IMPORTANT] > **Microphone permission required for Voice Chat.** When you activate voice chat (dashboard → Game Configs → Voice Chat), BannerHub will prompt for the **Microphone** permission. You must allow it for in-game calls to work — without it the call falls back to opening in your browser. > [!IMPORTANT] > **Notification permission required.** BannerHub will prompt for the **Post Notifications** permission when you start your first Epic, GOG, or Amazon download. You must allow it to receive download progress and completion notifications. > [!IMPORTANT] > **Files access required for SD card storage.** To save games to your SD card, BannerHub must be granted the **Files and Media** permission (Android Settings → Apps → BannerHub → Permissions → Files and media → Allow management of all files). Without it the SD card toggle has no effect. ## 🎙️ In-game voice chat (no Steam, no root) BannerHub now has a **built-in voice chat** you can use while in a game — **no Steam account, no root, no extra app**. Identity is just a **nickname** you pick plus a stable per-install id; there are no friends lists and no ringing. It's a **room model**: Create or Join a room by a short code, or share a join link. - **Two-gate activation.** A new **Voice Chat** item appears in the dashboard side menu under **Game Configs** → nickname field + **Check availability**. An **Activate** toggle stays disabled until the name is confirmed free; ticking it claims the name and turns the in-game pill on. - **In-game pill + room box.** A draggable edge pill opens a room box with the live **roster** (everyone's nickname), a call **timer**, and **Mute** / **Leave**. The pill stays pinned to the edge while the box drags freely, with a collapsible **opacity** slider. - **Cross-play with BannerHub v6.** Voice rides the **same WebRTC mesh** (BannerHub Cloudflare Worker + Cloudflare Realtime TURN) that BannerHub v6 uses, so a **BannerHub user and a BannerHub v6 user can share the same room and talk**, in either direction. Room codes use the same format on both builds. **Device-confirmed both ways across different networks.** ## 🔊 Per-game PC Audio Settings (recording-compatible mode) A new **PC Audio Settings** entry in each game's popup options menu (right after **PC Vibration Settings**) fixes **silent screen recordings**. Stock GameHub loads `module-aaudio-sink` with no performance-mode argument, so PulseAudio opens a `LOW_LATENCY` AAudio stream the framework grants as exclusive MMAP — which **bypasses the mixer that screen recorders tap**. The result: recordings capture video but **no game audio** whenever the in-game audio driver is PulseAudio (ALSA records fine). - **PulseAudio mode: Low latency (default) / Recording-compatible.** Recording-compatible appends `pm=0` to the sink line → `PERFORMANCE_MODE_NONE` → the stream stays on the mixer so screen recordings capture in-game sound. - The active game's `gameId` is resolved from the live `WineActivity`'s `WineActivityData` field (the 5.3.5 launch intent carries no `gameId` extra), and the setting lives in `pc_g_setting`, so **Export / Import Config** carries it automatically. ### Tracking BannerHub updates - **This repo's [Releases page](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases)** — watch the repo to get notified. - **[Obtainium](https://github.com/ImranR98/Obtainium)** — add `https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub` as an app source with **"Reconcile version string with version detected from OS"** enabled. ### Upgrading - **v3.7.5 → v3.8.0:** install the matching variant directly over your existing install. No certificate change, no data loss. - **v3.7.0 or earlier → v3.8.0:** install normally. If the signing certificate differs, uninstall first.

releases.name: Bannerhub v3.7.5
releases.body:
> [!WARNING]
> **Only install official stable releases** (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or raw CI artifacts — pre-release builds use a different package name (`com.tencent.ig`) and cannot be upgraded to stable without uninstalling first.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Notification permission required.** BannerHub will prompt for the **Post Notifications** permission when you start your first Epic, GOG, or Amazon download. You must allow it to receive download progress and completion notifications. Without it, you will not be notified when downloads finish or fail.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Files access required for SD card storage.** To save games to your SD card, BannerHub must be granted the **Files and Media** permission. Go to **Android Settings → Apps → BannerHub → Permissions → Files and media** and set it to **Allow management of all files** (or equivalent on your device). Without this permission the SD card toggle will have no effect.

## Offline launch for imported PC games

**Imported PC games (and any non-Steam game source) now launch offline.**

Pre-3.7.5, putting the device in airplane mode or on a network without internet and tapping an imported PC game would abort the launch with `Game configuration file download failed`. Steam library games had a partial offline bypass already; the other four game source types (imported via the PC Game Manager, local-file import, server-downloaded, and unknown) did not.

v3.7.5 adds a no-network skip on the three setup tasks that previously required a network call: `GameConfigDownloadTask` (fetches per-game env config from the server), `SetGameRecommConfigTask` (applies server-pushed recommendations), and `DependencyInstallTask` (checks the server for new dep versions). When `NetworkUtils.r()` reports no network, those tasks are skipped and the launch proceeds with whatever components, container, and Wine version were set up the last time the game launched online.

In practice:

- **Any game that has launched online at least once will now launch offline.** Wine boots, the game runs, the previously-applied container settings persist.
- **Steam library games unaffected.** Their existing offline support is unchanged.
- **Self-healing on reconnect.** When you come back online, the next launch hits the full pipeline and re-syncs any server-pushed recommendations or dep updates. The offline window doesn't accumulate drift.
- **No behavior change online.** All three patches are pure additions wrapped in `if no network: skip; else: original code`. The online path runs the original 3.7.4 logic byte-for-byte. Verified by code review and pre1→pre4 device iteration.

### Known limitations

1. **First-ever launches still require network** — if a game has never been set up at all, no components/container are on disk to fall back to. This release only fixes the second-and-later offline launches.
2. **Stale per-game settings** — if you change a per-game setting (Box64 variant, container, etc.) and then go offline before the next launch, the offline launch uses the previous on-disk setup. Workaround: relaunch once online after changing settings, then go offline.
3. **Server-pushed component recommendations skipped offline** — practical impact nil for games already working; deferred to the next online launch.

### Tracking BannerHub updates

- **This GitHub repo's [Releases page](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases)** — watch the repo to get notified
- **[Obtainium](https://github.com/ImranR98/Obtainium)** — add `https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub` as an app source with **"Reconcile version string with version detected from OS"** enabled

### Upgrading

- v3.7.4 → v3.7.5: install the matching variant directly over your existing install. No certificate change, no data loss.
- v3.7.0 or earlier → v3.7.5: install normally. If the signing certificate differs, uninstall first.

releases.name: Bannerhub v3.7.4
releases.body:
> [!WARNING]
> **Only install official stable releases** (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or raw CI artifacts — pre-release builds use a different package name (`com.tencent.ig`) and cannot be upgraded to stable without uninstalling first.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Notification permission required.** BannerHub will prompt for the **Post Notifications** permission when you start your first Epic, GOG, or Amazon download. You must allow it to receive download progress and completion notifications.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Files access required for SD card storage.** To save games to your SD card, BannerHub must be granted the **Files and Media** permission (**Android Settings → Apps → BannerHub → Permissions → Files and media → Allow management of all files**). Without it the SD card toggle has no effect.

## What's new in v3.7.4

One focused fix: **PC Vibration / Rumble no longer breaks x86_64 / Box64 game launches.**

### The problem (v3.7.0 – v3.7.3)

The PC Vibration / Rumble feature added in v3.7.0 loaded a small helper library, `libevshim.so`, into the Wine process via `LD_PRELOAD` to keep sustained rumble alive (SDL2 otherwise auto-stops the motor after ~1 second). That worked on arm64 / FEX containers — but on **Box64**, mapping that extra library into the game's address space corrupted the dynarec and made **every x86_64 game launch die at startup** with `c000007b` / `wine has died`. If you ran x86_64 titles under Box64 on v3.7.0–v3.7.3, this is the regression that was killing your games.

### The fix (v3.7.4) — preload-free

v3.7.4 removes `libevshim.so` entirely. **No extra library is mapped into Wine anymore**, so the crash class is eliminated. Sustained rumble is preserved by patching every `winebus.so` in the container **on disk** so SDL2's ~1 s rumble auto-expiry never fires:

- **aarch64-unix** and **x86_64-unix (Box64)** byte patterns are both handled, with the rumble-*stop* call sites left untouched so release-on-let-go still works.
- Safety net: if the x86_64 pattern ever misses on a future Proton, the unmatched `winebus.so` is dumped to the app's external files directory for re-derivation rather than failing silently.
- Everything else about the feature is unchanged — independent low/high motors, single-motor blend, instant release, multi-controller auto-wake, the per-game **PC Vibration Settings** dialog, and Export/Import config. Same continuous-rumble behavior, no library injected, no launch regression.

### Validation

Device-verified before promotion:

- **Launch regression cleared:** Dead Cells and ULTRAKILL launch cleanly on **Box64 "Extreme"**, DOOMBLADE on **FEX** — no `c000007b`, no crash, no dump.
- **Dirt 3** (x86_64 / Box64): a clean **~26-minute** session with **sustained continuous rumble** confirmed in-game (no more ~1 s SDL cutoff). Wine log showed zero `evshim` tags (library fully removed, as expected) and no x86_64 dump fallback — the on-disk pattern matched this Proton build.
- **Manual in-container check:** rumble was also tested directly by running **`GameConTest.exe`** (Microsoft's XInput GameController Tester) manually inside the container, driving the low and high motors on demand to confirm the on-disk `winebus.so` patch holds the motor through sustained input independent of any specific game.

## Upgrading

- **v3.7.x → v3.7.4:** install the matching variant directly over your existing install. No certificate change, no data loss. All vibration settings (`bh_vibration_*` in `pc_g_setting<gameId>`) carry over untouched.
- **Earlier → v3.7.4:** install normally. If the signing certificate differs, uninstall first.
- Variants with different package names cannot replace each other in-place.

## Credits

- **PC-accurate Vibration / Rumble** — original feature by **[TideGear](https://github.com/TideGear)** via [PR #80](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/pull/80), rebased from [GameNative PR #1214](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1214) by the same author.
- **v3.7.4 x86_64 / Box64 preload-free rework** — also by **[TideGear](https://github.com/TideGear)**, contributed as [PR #91 "Make Vibration Fix Compatible With x86_64 Containers"](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/pull/91). The PR was closed on 2026-05-16 after its code was adopted directly into BannerHub with TideGear's explicit permission.
- AI Frame Generation engine by the **GameHub team**; the v3.7.3 GOG reliability overhaul ported from [`utkarshdalal/GameNative`](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative). All v3.7.x features remain active.

**Please go support GameNative: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative**

## Community Game Configs

The in-app Community Game Configs browser currently serves **1,321 games** with **4,840 device-specific configs** contributed by the community.

## Installation

See https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub#installation for CPU/variant selection and step-by-step setup.

---

For the v3.7.0 baseline (AI Frame Generation menu, PC Vibration / Rumble) see the [v3.7.0 release notes](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases/tag/v3.7.0); for the GOG multi-CDN overhaul + FrameGen onResume fix see [v3.7.3](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases/tag/v3.7.3).

releases.name: Bannerhub v3.7.3
releases.body: > [!WARNING] > **Only install official stable releases** (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or raw CI artifacts — pre-release builds use a different package name (`com.tencent.ig`) and cannot be upgraded to stable without uninstalling first. > [!IMPORTANT] > **Notification permission required.** BannerHub will prompt for the **Post Notifications** permission when you start your first Epic, GOG, or Amazon download. You must allow it to receive download progress and completion notifications. Without it, you will not be notified when downloads finish or fail. > [!IMPORTANT] > **Files access required for SD card storage.** To save games to your SD card, BannerHub must be granted the **Files and Media** permission. Go to **Android Settings → Apps → BannerHub → Permissions → Files and media** and set it to **Allow management of all files** (or equivalent on your device). Without this permission the SD card toggle will have no effect. ## What's new in v3.7.3 Two independent reliability fixes, both device-confirmed. ### 1. GOG downloads — multi-CDN failover + resumable PARTIAL state GOG ships every depot through several CDN endpoints — typically `cdn.gog.com`, `gog-cdn-fastly.gog.com`, `gog-cdn-akamai.gog.com`, and a couple of region-specific mirrors. Up until v3.7.2 BannerHub picked one and abandoned the whole download if that CDN returned 403 / 404 / 5xx on a single file. v3.7.3 fixes this top-to-bottom. - **Automatic failover.** Each file probes every CDN advertised in the depot manifest in order; the first one that succeeds wins. A single bad endpoint no longer kills the install. - **CDN picker on the install dialog.** A new "CDN" row sits alongside size and storage on the install-confirm dialog. Leave it on **Auto** (default) for round-robin failover, or pin a specific CDN if your network plays better with one — useful when an ISP throttles a particular hostname or when GOG's own routing sends you to a degraded edge. - **↻ Refresh button.** Tap the refresh icon on the picker to re-fetch the live CDN list from GOG without dismissing the dialog. Use this if you opened the dialog, walked away for a while, and CDNs have rotated. - **PARTIAL install state.** When a download is interrupted (cancel, kill, OOM, network drop), BannerHub now records `gog_state_=PARTIAL` with the in-flight file index + byte offset. Re-tapping install picks up from the same point instead of starting over. All three GOG UIs (library list, library grid, game-detail dialog) now share one install-state model — the checkmark / Add Game / Resume affordances are consistent everywhere. - **Diagnostic toast.** When a file does fail across every CDN, the error toast now names the file, the last CDN tried, and the HTTP code. The same line is also appended to `bh_gog_debug.txt` (in your app's external files directory) so you can copy-paste an exact failure into a bug report. **Device-confirmed during pre-release testing:** POSTAL 2 (multi-CDN fallback survived a single-CDN failure mid-install), Citadel Remonstered (468/468 files clean on Auto), CDN picker UI, ↻ Refresh button. ### 2. AI Frame Generation — resume reliability + cleaner gear dialog Three issues from v3.7.0 / v3.7.1 / v3.7.2 are addressed in one bundle. - **Frame-gen no longer "lies" after resume.** Previously, the `gamescope.control` bytes were only written on first launch. Pressing Home or locking the screen and resuming the game left the sidebar switch saying **ON** while the AI overlay was silently dead — your FPS would drop back to native without the switch ever changing state. The launch hook now also runs on `WineActivity.onResume`, so the overlay survives every backgrounding cycle. If the sidebar switch says ON, AI frame generation is **actually on**. - **Gear icon hides when the switch is OFF.** The gear button next to the sidebar switch now defaults to hidden and only appears when AI Frame Generation is enabled. Matches the existing pattern used by RTS controls in the same sidebar. - **Dialog cleanup — game view stays visible.** The settings dialog no longer paints a full-screen black background over your game. It now uses Android's translucent dim with the game rendering live underneath at 60% dim, the same way native system dialogs look. The dialog has three controls — Preset slider, Custom flow scale slider, and a blue **Close** button — and tap-outside also dismisses. The multiplier picker is gone (it was always 2× on tested hardware anyway; the value is now hardcoded) and the redundant in-dialog Enable switch is gone (the sidebar switch is the single source of truth). Net result: faster to operate, easier on the eyes, no behavior loss. ## Upgrading - **v3.7.2 → v3.7.3:** install the matching variant directly over your existing install. No certificate change, no data loss. GOG library + install state are preserved (`bh_gog_prefs` keys are forward-compatible — old completed installs read as `INSTALLED`, no migration needed). FrameGen settings (`bh_framegen.xml`) carry over; the removed `multiplier` key is simply ignored. - **v3.7.1 or earlier → v3.7.3:** install normally. If the signing certificate differs, uninstall first. - Variants with different package names cannot replace each other in-place. ## Credits - **GOG download reliability overhaul** ported from upstream [`utkarshdalal/GameNative`](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative): multi-CDN [PR #1220](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1220) + [PR #1219](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1219) by **Utkarsh Dalal** (with co-author **Jeremy Bernstein** on #1219), CDN picker [PR #1215](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1215) by **Bart Zaalberg**, install-state PARTIAL [PR #1277](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1277) by **Joshua Tam**. - **AI Frame Generation onResume + dialog cleanup** ported from [Bannerhub-Lite PR #5](https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerhub-Lite/pull/5) by **[teldommm](https://github.com/teldommm)**. - All v3.7.0 features (AI Frame Generation engine itself by the **GameHub team**, PC-accurate Vibration / Rumble by [TideGear](https://github.com/TideGear) via [GameNative PR #1214](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1214)) remain active. **Please go support GameNative: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative** --- For details on the v3.7.0 baseline (AI Frame Generation menu, PC Vibration / Rumble), see the [v3.7.0 release notes](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases/tag/v3.7.0). For the v3.7.1 / v3.7.2 upgrade-prompt stubs, see the [v3.7.1](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases/tag/v3.7.1) and [v3.7.2](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases/tag/v3.7.2) release notes.

releases.name: Bannerhub v3.7.2
releases.body:
> [!WARNING]
> **Only install official stable releases** (like this one). Do NOT install pre-releases or raw CI artifacts — pre-release builds use a different package name (`com.tencent.ig`) and cannot be upgraded to stable without uninstalling first.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Notification permission required.** BannerHub will prompt for the **Post Notifications** permission when you start your first Epic, GOG, or Amazon download. You must allow it to receive download progress and completion notifications. Without it, you will not be notified when downloads finish or fail.

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Files access required for SD card storage.** To save games to your SD card, BannerHub must be granted the **Files and Media** permission. Go to **Android Settings → Apps → BannerHub → Permissions → Files and media** and set it to **Allow management of all files** (or equivalent on your device). Without this permission the SD card toggle will have no effect.

## Hotfix for v3.7.1

**This is a hotfix release.** No new features. All v3.7.1 functionality (and everything earlier) is preserved.

### What changed

**The launch-time auto-update dialog no longer probes upstream GameHub either.**

v3.7.1 stubbed the *manual* upgrade probe — the Settings → About → "Check Update" row. But there is a **second** call site for the same upgrade endpoint: an auto-fired dialog that pops on every cold start, routed through `ApkUpdateUtils.checkUpdate` rather than the Settings row. In v3.7.1 that auto-dialog was still asking GameHub's servers for an upgrade on every launch, getting back 6.0.1 / `versionCode 110`, and offering it to the user — i.e. offering to replace BannerHub with stock GameHub.

v3.7.2 closes the second mouth. The same singleton (`AppUpgradeRepo.b()`) is now neutralised at *both* call sites with the same anchor-patch shape — its suspend invocation is replaced with `const/4 p1, 0x0`, so the result resumes as `null` and the existing "no update" branch (`if-eqz`) is always taken.

In v3.7.2:

- **No upgrade dialog on app launch.** Cold-starting the app no longer pops the GameHub upgrade prompt.
- Settings → About → "Check Update" — unchanged from v3.7.1, still reads **"Already the latest version"** in gray.
- Logcat shows **zero** `getAppUpgradeApk` traffic from either surface.

The patch is one extra 5-instruction smali edit in `ApkUpdateUtils$checkUpdate$1$1.smali`, mirrored across both `build.yml` (stable) and `build-quick.yml` (pre-release). Same singleton as v3.7.1, different inner class. No behavior change for any other surface — login, store, prices, achievements, Steam card, ratings, downloads, AI Frame Generation, Vibration / Rumble, EOS authentication are all untouched.

### Tracking BannerHub updates

Since both in-app upgrade surfaces are now silent, use either:

- **This GitHub repo's [Releases page](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases)** — watch the repo to get notified
- **[Obtainium](https://github.com/ImranR98/Obtainium)** — add `https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub` as an app source with **"Reconcile version string with version detected from OS"** enabled

### Upgrading

- v3.7.1 → v3.7.2: install the matching variant directly over your existing install. No certificate change, no data loss.
- v3.7.0 or earlier → v3.7.2: install normally. If the signing certificate differs, uninstall first.
- Variants with different package names cannot replace each other in-place.

### Credits

Hotfix patch developed and tested by [@The412Banner](https://github.com/The412Banner). AI Frame Generation engine (still active from v3.7.0) is the work of the **GameHub team**; PC-accurate Vibration / Rumble (still active from v3.7.0) is from [TideGear's PR #80](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/pull/80) via [GameNative PR #1214](https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative/pull/1214). **Please go support GameNative: https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative**

---

For the full v3.7.0 feature set (AI Frame Generation menu, PC Vibration / Rumble, and everything earlier), see the [v3.7.0 release notes](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases/tag/v3.7.0). For details on the original (v3.7.1) Check Update row stub, see the [v3.7.1 release notes](https://github.com/The412Banner/BannerHub/releases/tag/v3.7.1).

releases.name: Gamehub 6.0 - Bannerhub API - No Login - Muted UI
0.5.3 preview
releases.body:
# 0.5.3 preview

## X360 Mobile — Update

This update introduces setting priority fixes, new UI sound effects, and network compatibility workarounds for offline gameplay.

### Settings Management

* **Configuration Priority**: Fixed the priority order of settings. Game-specific configurations now correctly overwrite global settings as intended.

### UI Improvements

* **Audio Feedback**: Added custom sound effects to the UI. These are designed to evoke the feel of the original console interface while remaining distinct to avoid legal issues.

### Network Compatibility

* **Graceful Fallbacks**: Implemented semantic fixes and function workarounds for titles with online components. Network checks now fail gracefully, allowing games that previously blocked progression to continue in offline mode.

releases.name: 0.5.2 public
releases.body:
# 0.5.2 public

## X360 Mobile — Update

This update focuses on compatibility, UI cleanup, driver handling, Frame Interpolation improvements, and safer Vulkan cache management.

### Compatibility Improvements

* **UBWC Hinting**: Added a UBWC hint to improve compatibility on Android systems such as HyperOS, OneUI, and other customized vendor ROMs.

### UI Improvements

* **UI Fixes**: Several fixes have been made, especially around the hint bar and its buttons.
* **Code Refactoring**: Internal UI code has been refactored to improve consistency, reduce edge cases, and ensure future stability.

### Driver Management Improvements

* **Touch Input**: Driver handling with touch input has been improved.
* **Manual Deletion**: It is now possible to delete a custom driver by long-pressing it.

### Frame Interpolation Improvements

* **Feature Renaming**: “Frame Gen” has been renamed to “Frame Interpolation” to better describe its functionality.
* **Visual Quality**: Quality has been slightly improved, and ghosting during fast scene changes has been fixed.

### Vulkan Cache Improvements

* **Driver Isolation**: Caches are now separated per game and per driver, preventing issues when switching custom drivers.
* **Manual Cleanup**: It is now possible to clear/delete Vulkan caches manually.

releases.name: 0.5.1 public
releases.body:
# X360 Mobile — Update

This update improves game launch diagnostics and adds full SAF library mode support.

## Improved Launch Logging

Game initialization logging is now more detailed, making instant crashes and boot failures easier to investigate.

This should help identify issues related to file access, metadata detection, content loading, configuration, or early startup failures.

## Full SAF Library Mode

SAF library mode is now fully supported.

New features:

- Multi-folder library re-scan through the Android file picker
- Direct launch from `content://` URIs without copying files
- Metadata and Title ID detection for SAF games
- Per-game settings support for SAF-added games

SAF mode is useful on Android systems with stricter file access behavior, such as ColorOS, OxygenOS, OriginOS, and other heavily customized Android builds. **If your current setup already works correctly with standard file access, you may not need to switch to SAF mode.**

## How To Enable SAF

Go to:

`Settings > Emulation > Game File Access`

Then select:

`Android File Picker (SAF)`

releases.name: 0.5 public
releases.body:
# X360 Mobile - First Public Release (v0.5)

Welcome to the very first public release of **X360 Mobile**! This version represents a major milestone in bringing Xbox 360 emulation to the Android platform.

### Highlights & Features
* **Initial Public Launch:** The emulator is now available for public testing.
* **Turnip Driver Support:** Full compatibility with the latest Turnip/Adreno drivers for Snapdragon devices.

### Known Issues
As this is an early build, please expect the following:
* Performance varies significantly depending on the device chipset.
* Some graphical glitches and crashes may occur in specific titles.
* Audio synchronization is still a work in progress.

### Installation
1. Download the **X360_0.5_public.apk** attached below.
2. Ensure you have your own legally dumped game files (ISO/XEX).
3. For best results on Snapdragon, use the an adeguate Turnip driver..

### Special Thanks
A huge thank you to the community for the incredible support during the development phase and for helping us reach this public launch on **May 31, 2026**.

---
*Disclaimer: X360 Mobile is an independent project and is not affiliated with Microsoft or the Xbox brand.*

releases.name: The future of star - What will it become?
releases.body:
Hello everyone. I'm writing this to inform all of you on the current status of star, and what it will be and if its actually getting continued.

Star will be continued, yes, but also no. It's going to be a fresh new start, because everything was ruined because of my actions and how I acted with the project, along with the team members. I apologize for my actions and I hope I can be forgiven. The project is now called WinHub, and in a little bit, development will start.

So, here is a little list of things you should know:
- The star development continues! (now under WinHub)
- The focus on next release will be mostly Quality of Life features. This means fixing long standing bugs, but more importantly, adding a lot of new features.
- The Vegas implementation will be severely improved with new features, configurations and more.
- The experience will be severely improved. I'll try my best to make it the most user friendly possible.

-----

Now, this is pretty much everything that's in my mind right now. I will probably update this later if I remember something else.

Have a great day everyone!
- Hernandez / yaywoohoo

releases.name: star 1.3 - UI redesigns, Vegas Engine built in, New FPS Counter, New in-game menu redesign
releases.body: **After a lot of previews, star 1.3 is here. We had some juicy changes made! But first of all, credits!** - @jacojayy: All of the changes, excluding the Vegas work. - @leegao: Helping in Vegas. It was a pleasure to work and chat with you, hope we will do it again in the future! - @isygold: Developing Vegas ### NOTE - The Vegas FrameGen still doesnt work. - When installing Proton / Wine versions, do not panic. It will install over time, just wait! ## Changelog - Redesigned the in-game ui: - The in-game ui menu now has a modern and sleek look, making the experience way more fresh and easier. - Redesigned FPS Counter - The FPS Counter is now horizontal by default and can be customized via the in game menu. - Integrated Vegas into star. - Removed contents tab: - Contents can now be installed directly in the container / shortcut settings. - Revamped shortcut tab - Made the shortcut image size 2:3 - Removed the appearance tab to make the experience less buggy - Defaulted the theme to AMOLED. - Added the OneUI / HyperOS fix to the Wrapper settings. - There is no need to choose a custom Turnip version or put the enviroment variable. Just enable the toggle! - Contents download speed has been made slightly faster.

releases.name: v1.3.0
releases.body:
Additions: 
- [Preference] Day, Night, Auto theme.
- [Preference] Theme color palette.
- [Preference] Which screen to launch on. (Library, Downloads, or Friends).
- Chatting with friends, with message history, emoticon and sticker support. 
- Friend profiles, with basic friend management.
- Option to view external web links in app or externally with a browser of choice.
- Game app screen shows basic information.
- More library filer options, which are retained. 

Fixes: 
- Profile dialog works better for long names now.
- Crash when null messages are logged. 
- Crashes when a failed sign in occurred, such as invalid credentials. 
- Correct display of install/run button and download progress for apps. Thanks @Producdevity
- Add smooth transitions for download state. Thanks @Producdevity
- Friends list groups able to be collapsible now.
- Library scroll position should remember when navigating away.
- Library list optimization.
- Soft resize issue with on-screen keyboard is opened/closed.
- Rotating the screen closes some dialogs.
- Dialog list scrolling on small screen height. Thanks @Producdevity
- Themed app icon being oversized.
- Game app screen image loading shifting.

Changes: 
- Connection to steam now uses the Web Socket protocol.
- Login errors are shown in a snackbar.
- Various theme unification for light and dark mode.
- Various Compose tweaks.
- Access tokens now encrypted.
- Removed automatic app backup.
- Actively filter out Spacewar (game).
- Library filter options now is a bottom sheet modal.

Misc: 
- Add Github Actions for snapshot and validation builds.
- Update readme.
- Dependency updates. Now using JavaSteam 1.6.0.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/oxters168/Pluvia/compare/v1.2.0...v1.3.0

releases.body:
## Changelog (Based on Official v11.1 Hotfix 2)

(Go to Official Winlator GitHub to get latest winlator 11.1 final)
---
### **Ajay_Custom_Components**
You can install various components—such as d7vk, dxwrapper_ddraw, dxwrapper_d3d8, d3d8to9, and custom—directly from the `D:/winlator/Ajay_Custom_Components` directory. If a newer version of these components becomes available, simply replace the existing DLLs in that folder to update them.

These components will be installed upon container launch, not shortcut launch.

### **Drive Path Update**
* **Path Migration:** Changed `drive E` to `drive K` for `installed_components`.
* **Manual Installation:** Supported manual copying of components directly to `drive K\installed_components\`.

### **System & Environment**
* **Automated Symlink:** Mapped `C:\users` to `Z:\home\Ajay_drive\users` to prevent save data loss when switching containers.
* **Environment Variables:** Added some env variables.
* **Box64rc Optimization:** Updated config for trainer compatibility and added "trainer" keyword logic for auto-application.

### **Quick Access (Start Menu)**
* **Components Downloader:** Added GUI app for one-click downloads of **Box64**, **Turnip**, **DXVK** etc.
* **Latest box64rc Downloader:** Added option to download the latest **Ajay.box64rc** instantly.
---
#### Note : Don't Change drive K path.

* **Drive path Update:** Changed drive E path to drive K for installed_components , You can copy box64,  turnip etc to drive K.

* **Environment Variables:** Added new environment variables for improved configuration.

---

### Final Update
* **SymLink Fix:** Resolved an issue causing the SymLink process to repeat unnecessarily.
* **Autorun.exe:** The application now runs without a console window.
* **Bug Fixes:** General stability improvements and minor bug resolutions.

### How to run ajau3 Script for downloading latest dxvk and turnip etc ?
Just install Ajay Prefix Pro then double click on it.
Requirements : Drive K should be mounted of this path -  `/data/data/com.winlator/files`

### How to run the trainer with game performance presets?
Simply include the word **"trainer"** in the filename of your game trainer or Cheat Engine application.

### Winlator.Ajay.Components.Downloader.ajau3 -
this program can be run after installing Ajay Prefix Pro. It can download latest installable components (box64 , turnip , wined3d , dxvk , vkd3d etc).

---
*Note: This release focuses exclusively on streamlining and automating the setup process. Do not close the container if any of the following processes are active in Task Manager: autorun.exe, cmd.exe, wget.exe, xcopy.exe, or 7z.exe.*

# hakuX v0.3.0

## Performance
- GPU overhead reduction: batched surface downloads, eliminated redundant image transitions, no-op transition guard
- Texture cache doubled and made configurable — fixes texture pop-in in open-world games
- Native BC1/BC2 compressed texture upload; GPU compute DXT5 decompression
- NV2A fast-path expansion for high-frequency methods
- VkSampler cache, NEON blit optimization, optimized descriptor management

## Stability
- Fix 30fps trap — games no longer get stuck at half speed after temporary drops
- Fix pipeline exhaustion crash, surface eviction corruption, XISO conversion issues
- Fix RCU crash on quick restart, NOP assert, shader binding crash
- Xbox kernel crash detection with diagnostic output

## Audio
- Audio subsystem aligned with X1 BOX

## Texture Modding
- Dump game textures as PNG for texture pack creation

## Settings & UI & Networking
- Per-game settings with visual override highlighting
- Configurable texture cache size, skip occlusion queries, skip boot animation
- Xbox dashboard management, EEPROM editor, Insignia networking (ported from X1 BOX)
- Debug log export

## Known issues:
- Some games are broken on both hakuX and X1 BOX, like Night Caster. This is under investigation and probably related to something fundamental in the initial porting to Android
- Fable has fundamental issues made worse by some of the optimizations on hakuX. While probing the root cause added a disgusting skip.

releases.name: hakuX 0.2.1
releases.body:
## hakuX v0.2.1

### Bug Fixes
- **Mitigates HDD corruption** — restore block device flush on app terminate and background to persist QCOW2 metadata
- **Fix game freeze on TB flush** — disable post-flush rewarm on Android which could hang on self-modified code
- **Fix on-screen controller hidden on some devices** — filter out virtual/internal devices (accelerometers, gyroscopes)

### Improvements
- **Simplified FPS overlay** — shows only FPS count

releases.name: hakuX 0.2.0
releases.body:
## hakuX v0.2.0

### New Features
- **OpenGL ES renderer** — experimental alternative to Vulkan, selectable in Graphics settings (ported from X1 BOX)
- **EEPROM editor** — change Xbox language, video standard, resolution modes, aspect ratio, refresh rate (ported from X1 BOX)
- **Settings index** — reorganized into 4 sections: hakuX Data, Graphics, Debug, EEPROM
- **System file pickers** — change MCPX ROM, Flash ROM, HDD image from settings
- **HDD export** — save a copy of the Xbox HDD to any folder
- **Games folder in settings** — moved from library screen to settings
- **Game search** — search bar with live filtering in game library
- **Pull-to-refresh** — swipe down to rescan games folder
- **XISO progress bar** — real progress tracking across copy/convert/save phases

### Improvements
- **Controller overlay** — D-pad/stick repositioned, LS/RS as separate buttons, menu button, swipe-up gesture, trigger axis fix (-1.0 for release), per-pointer tracking  (ported from X1 BOX)
- **Pause menu** — actually pauses emulation, simplified UI
- **NEON optimizations** — S3TC decompression and texture swizzle (benefits both renderers)

### Bug Fixes
- Fix TB cache crash when FP settings change between sessions
- Fix pause/resume not working from in-game menu
- Fix GLES geometry shader crash (gl_PointSize unavailable without extension)
- Fix GLES shader version detection (was hardcoded to 320, now auto-detected)
- Fix shader link failure crash (graceful fallback instead of assert)
- Fix shared GLSL changes breaking VK (gated behind renderer check)

* Added microphone support for Nintendo DS
* Brand new touch controls
* Add immersive mode (dynamic background color)
* Slightly improved HD mode
* Added quicksave/quickload gamepad shortcuts
* Added support for 16Kb pages
* Added best-effort saves when transitioning to background
* Updated all cores and databases
* Various UI/UX improvements
Improved HD mode quality and performances
* Fix cores not downloaded in the Github build

* Migrate mobile UI to Jetpack Compose
* Improved HD upscaling algorithm
* Updated cores
* Fix UI issues in Home
* Minor fixes

* Introduced a new HD mode
* Updated cores
* Updated databases

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.4.1
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.4.1

What's new:

Animated PlayStation-3 XMB library background. The library now has the live flowing XMB wave as its default backdrop, the way the iOS build does. Custom backgrounds still work, pick any image, GIF or WebP and it overrides the wave, reset returns to it.

On-screen stats hotkey now CYCLES instead of a plain on/off: Full --> Minimal --> Custom --> Off. The same modes are on the in-game On-Screen menu, and your choice is remembered. Hotkeys also are an option in the On-screen all settings tab.

Library music controls: a volume slider (now defaults to 15%), and you
can now pick your own track, any audio file from your device, with a reset-to-default. 

Display Zoom (Settings --> Fixes): one slider that zooms into the picture,
trimming every edge equally so it fills more of the screen without stretching
much simpler than the four manual crop sliders for tablets and modern screens.

Networking works again while fast-forwarding. 2.6.4 started handing the console public DNS servers it had to reach over a real network round-trip, which fast-forward outran, DNS now behaves as it did before.

Games no longer come back to a black screen (with audio) when relaunched on some Qualcomm Adreno devices. The Vulkan display surface is now rebuilt cleanly instead of getting stuck.

The replacement-texture cache was too small on higher-RAM devices and kept evicting a pack that would have fit it now scales to half of RAM (up to 6 GB instead of 2GB) so big packs stay usable.

Added more downloadable controller skins: Xbox, Burnout 3, Black, and more! 

Credits:

XMB background ported from linkev's
PlayStation-3-XMB. Controller skins by @bagasromadon.

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.4
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.4

What's new:
 
Library music. The game library now plays an ambient track, like a console dashboard. It stops the moment a game boots, stays quiet if something else is already playing audio, and can be turned off in Settings --> App.

Download controller skins in-app. Settings --> Skins now browses the community skin repository with a preview image for each pack, and installs with one tap.

Added an On-screen keyboard hotkey. Bind a button to raise your device's keyboard over a running game and type into the emulated USB keyboard, for titles with in-game chat. Requires Emulate USB Keyboard ON in Network tab.

Added Play time and last played, shown in a game's Info tab. These totals have been recorded all along, just not exposed in the UI, so existing users already have hours waiting there.

More UI themes: Material You (Android 12+), an RGB rainbow cycle, a custom accent color picker, and Cyan, Orange, Red, Green, Pink, Purple, and more!

Shoulder buttons switch settings categories, for controller users.

Per-tab settings reset, so a single settings page can be restored to defaults without touching the rest.

Portrait: an option to align the display to the top of the screen rather than
centering it, now the default.

Achievements now show whether an achievement is an active challenge.

Fixes:

OpenGL (ANGLE) works again. Helpful for Mali and other low-end users. 

True Crime: New York City now fixed thanks to @bmdhacks (may have fixed LA as well).

OutRun 2006, Jak 3, and Jan X graphical issues (memcard hang still remains, restart game to bypass for the moment), fixed by @bmdhacks (more games may have been fixed as well from these improvements, as was seen in 2.6.3). 

Texture packs, fixed an out-of-memory crash on large packs, and heavy stutter as a
pack loads. The replacement-texture cache is now bounded and uploads are paced  across frames rather than landing in one.

DEV9 Ethernet, adapter selection and DNS now use the Android sockets path, so online play can find an adapter again. 

Patch enable state no longer bleeds between games, patches are scoped per game.

Folder memory cards on a custom data folder no longer crash, and improvements to per-game memory cards. 

Re added option to create Memory card folder. 

Joy-Con support! 

Saving or loading a state no longer leaves a black screen. 

Launching a .cue file from another app, such as Cocoon, should work again, (please let me know). 

The CRT/TV shader can be switched back off, selecting None now takes effect instead of leaving the previous shader running.

Adreno: the colorWriteMask-with-depth-test workaround no longer applies to Turnip drivers, which do not need it, thanks @bmdhacks 

EE FPU: MAX.S/MIN.S operands are clamped at clamp modes >= 1, and add/sub
guard-bit emulation is now unconditional, both bring the arm64 recompiler in line with the interpreter thanks to @bmdhacks 

Performance:

COP2 transfer ops now go through the register allocator instead of flushing everything unconditionally thanks to @bmdhacks 

The COP2 VU0-sync seam is outlined into shared stubs, shrinking generated code.
fastmem LQC2/SQC2 no longer emit an unconditional call flush. thanks to @bmdhacks 

Credits

Library music by The Cynic Project (CC0).

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.3
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.3 

What's new: 

New EE/VU recompiler backend done by @bmdhacks
The entire CPU core rebuilt on a new ARM64 recompiler ported from upstream PCSX2. Faster and far more accurate, a whole class of "unfixable on ARM" bugs are simply gone. Confirmed: Ratchet: Deadlocked graphical corruption fixed, and other problem games run as they should Crash Twinsanity, NFL 2K5, VP2 and the rest of the problem list now run correctly. This update should help low-end users greatly. 

GS Multi-threading (optional) by @bmdhacks
Splits GS onto a dedicated back thread, run 2x/3x internal resolution without the frame dips. Settings --> Renderer --> GS Multi-threading (and the in-game quick menu). Off by default, restart to apply.

Known issues, fixes being worked on:

Jak X: Combat Racing, slightly regressed (memory-card save hang + some in-game graphics glitches). Being worked on. For the moment, if you get stuck on it saving a memory card, restart the game and the save file will now exist. 

True Crime: New York City, fully regressed (the EE FPU accuracy fix isn't in the new backend yet). Being worked on, but I wanted to get this out now. Let us know how it is!

releases.name: ARMSX2 2.6.2
releases.body:
ARMSX2 2.6.2 

Emulation Fixes: 

ESPN NFL 2K5 no longer freezes during early gameplay and now works as intended. 

Ratchet & Clank: Deadlocked, restored two COP2/VU0 sync fixes lost in the monorepo migration, plus a monotonic-clock guard that stopped the EE being slammed backwards. Game is now playable, although one graphical issue remains from my testing.

Folder memory cards work on a custom data folder again. Creating save data crashed or reported "format failed" for anyone whose data folder wasn't internal app storage. 

Texture Packs:

Importing a pack no longer freezes the app when using an SD card. A real pack is thousands of files over SAF, and the copy ran on the UI thread,  black screen, then "ARMSX2 isn't responding", worse on SD. Import and delete now run in the background with a live file count to let you know when it's completed. 

BC/DXT packs work on GPUs without hardware support. Devices that don't expose textureCompressionBC, Adreno 650-class hardware, and Mesa Turnip on any Adreno, silently dropped every texture in a pack. Those are now decoded on the CPU.

Texture Packs are now reachable in-game, from the pause menu, with a Restart Game to Apply button. Packs are only loaded at boot. 

The Texture Manager now shows which folder the emulator will actually read, and warns when no installed pack matches it. Booting a raw .ELF derives the folder name from the filename rather than the disc serial, which silently sent packs to a folder nothing scanned.

Memory card import now accepts folders instead of just zips of folders. Folder cards are directories, but the picker only accepted files, so a zipped card was renamed to .ps2 and read as unformatted. Folder cards can now be imported unpacked from a zip, with validation and a clear error instead of a bad card.

Settings & Behaviour:

Made more improvements to per-game settings, now apply when launching from a frontend (Cocoon, Daijisho, ES-DE). External launches passed no game identity, so every title fell back to global settings, including per-game memory cards and orientation.  (please report if this still misbehaves.)

Patches work with RetroAchievements Hardcore enabled again. Hardcore blocked all on-disk patch files in the new UI, while the bundled ones stayed active, so the same widescreen or bug-fix patch worked from our archive and silently died from disk. Cheats remain blocked.

Overscan Crop (Settings --> Fixes), trim pixels from any edge to hide garbage or black bars games leave in the area a CRT would have cut off. Measured in native pixels, so it behaves the same at any resolution. 

Custom OSD colours, including from the in-game menu. 

Support for Custom game names in the Info tab.

GameDB, merged community PRs from @XDarkFallenX

Rendering/perf fixes:

Marvel: Ultimate Alliance 1 & 2,  Everybody's Golf 4, Magna Carta: Tears of Blood, Rumble Racing, Atelier Iris 2 & 3, Baldur's Gate: Dark Alliance 1 & 2, Jak 3, Kingdom Hearts II, Downhill Domination, Driv3r, Black, God Hand, Musashi: Samurai Legend, Aeon Flux, Artonelico: Melody of Elemia

releases.name: Winlator bionic ref4ik mod v10 Hotfix
releases.body:
## Winlator Mod bionic — ref4ik v10 Hotfix 

**What's New in This Version:**


- Fixed VKD3D selection in settings.

- Fixed the issue in shortcut settings where it was impossible to select FEX on third-party Wine (arm64ec)

- Tightened the search for game cover art



<details>
<summary>← Click here to view full previous changelog</summary>

### Previous Major Updates

🟣 Fixed FPS counter  
🟣 Fixed error in driver loading menu  
🟣 Removed limitation — now all Turnip drivers are shown in every repository (not just the first 10)  
🟣 **AdrenoTools**: added option to export downloaded driver to device storage  
🟣 Added option to hide FEX/Box config files when one is selected  
🟣 Game shortcut icons now load from **Steam**  
🟣 **Adreno Settings**: added initial parameters (work in progress)  
🟣 Integrated **AdrenoTools Store** for extended driver functionality  
🟣 Introduced **Wrapper v2** — improved driver selection UI  
🟣 Added fast **WFM** implementation from de0ver  
🟣 Added full **Russian** language support  
🟣 Reworked input controls settings:  
  → Now you can add custom icons to controls  
  → Go to control profile editor → select icons → apply  
  → Long-press icon to remove  
🟣 Added modular **FPS counter** in xservermenu (select parameters & save config)  
🟣 Updated Task Manager: added GPU and battery temperature indicators  
🟣 Changed application color scheme  
🟣 FPS counter: fixed bugs + added dimming option  
🟣 Input controls: added slider for custom icon size  
🟣 Fixed crash when adding Wine Proton  
🟣 **Active Windows**: now you can open/close running programs (UI/functionality to be improved later)  
🟣 Added built-in **File Manager**  
🟣 New screen effects  
🟣 Some FEX-related changes  
🟣 Ported components from 0.3.7.1 (Fex2508–2509)  
🟣 Partially updated app theme  
🟣 Fixed multiple bugs  
🟣 Changes in graphics driver handling  
🟣 Added **ALSA reflector**  
🟣 Fixed frame rate issues  
🟣 Updated image installer  
🟣 Added gamepad detection  
🟣 Added URL content support  
🟣 Big **"Putre"** button for quick game settings  
🟣 Moved preset frame parameters into container  
🟣 Added more options in graphics driver settings  
🟣 Added hotkey-based manual export  
🟣 Fixed wallpaper adding  
🟣 Fixed fullscreen mode  
🟣 Fixed hotkey settings  
🟣 Increased custom icon limit: 27 → 1000  
🟣 Fixed conflict with simultaneous Termux installation  
🟣 Control settings: added option to disable button outline + transparency slider  
🟣 Various design improvements  
🟣 Significantly improved Active Windows behavior  
🟣 Greatly improved manual shortcut import  
🟣 Added **FEX** preset  
🟣 Added ability to install Windows components  
🟣 Added DXVK & Turnip download into container  
🟣 Added audio driver settings  
🟣 Improved FPS counter: new design, draggable, more size options  
🟣 Added FPS limiter  
🟣 Added app language selector in settings  
🟣 Added Box/FEX/etc. buttons for hotkey preset creation  
🟣 Added import/export for Box (.boxx) and FEX (.fexx) presets
- Added my custom modified **WFM**  
- Added ability to download **Wine** directly into the container  
- Updated **Content Manager**  
- Updated **Credits & Acknowledgements** section  
- Fixed multiple issues in translations  
- Added **Voltage** parameter to the FPS counter 
  - Icon Manager: Added support for ZIP packs and Input Bridge IPK icon packs.  (experimental)

- UI Update: The application has been fully migrated to Compose UI.

- Theme Manager: Added a new theme manager.

- Added SurfaceFlinger renderer and a choice between Vulkan/SurfaceFlinger in container settings.

- Removed wrappers v2–v6.

- Added Vulkan version selection to graphics settings.

- Added Adreno Turbo Mode (may slightly boost FPS when enabled).

- FPS Counter: Added customizable styles and color options for the FPS counter.

- Shortcut Settings: Updated the shortcut menu; navigating to the edit menu now opens a Steam game info page featuring screenshots, videos, ratings, etc.

- Updated the Steam interface and fixed various download bugs. Fixed Steam online status. Accelerated game downloads and increased the download queue limit. Added Steam Friends List with chat support. Added Steam Achievements viewer. Added library view modes: Grid, List, and Compact. Improved cloud saves. Fixed the "last played game" display in Steam.

- Sped up resource extraction on the first app launch.

- Removed several unused components, reducing the overall app size.

- Updated various system libraries.

- Integrated the MMKV library.

- Fixed CPU available extensions display.

- Improved Box/Fex component downloading inside container settings.

- Fixed a bug where enabling any screen effect caused the screen to split into two windows.

- Added an option to download drivers to the Adreno Tools store from StevenMXZ/Adreno-Tools-Drivers.

- Added GPU name selection + CustomName in CPU settings.

- Added Leegao wrapper.

- Added on-screen controls import from Input Bridge.  (experimental)

- Added selection and export of community configurations (experimental)

- Localization: Added Portuguese translation.
- Fixed various bugs in the FPS counter  
- Interface improvements and polishing   
- Added Chinese language  
- Added a new HDR effect to screen effects  
- Improved active windows  
- Fixed control settings  
- Fixed some bugs in themes  
- Fixed some minor bugs 
- - Added the ability to add your own custom repository with drivers in Adrenotools

- Added gyroscope control

- Added update check

- Fixed some bugs

- Added download buttons for Box and FEX in the container and in the shortcut settings

- Added import and export of container settings configuration

- Updated DXVK settings

- Added a file manager for the container

- Added animation selection

- Added GPU load parameter and frame time graph to the FPS counter

- Added pause functionality when minimizing the app (the container is no longer destroyed, but paused instead)

**Removed Features:**  
- Magnifier  
- Touch helper function  

**Minor Optimizations:** Various small performance and smoothness improvements.

- Viewing and removing shared installed components

- Fixed touch control disappearing after 5 seconds #9 

- Fixed downloading and caching of game covers

- Fixed UI bugs

- Improved on-screen controls editor

- Added frame generation from If the frame generator does not work on the first container launch, try restarting the container.

- Updated FPS counter

- Added experimental Steam games launch
- Added support for animated covers for games

- Added Relative Mouse option from the container to the control settings

- Added the ability to set a custom icon for a game

- Added BCn settings from implementation Winlator Bionic 

- Improved File Explorer — now you can create a game shortcut directly from it

- Interface changes: moved menus, "Installed Components" section, and Gamepad Test

- Removed Big Picture menu from the project and cleaned up other unused code

- Created a new SystemSensorPaths class - that contains all paths to the device's system sensors and centralized all sensor queries 

- Replaced the GL renderer with a Vulkan renderer. Old GL effects were removed, all effects were ported to custom Vulkan fragment shaders; added a Vulkan post-processing pipeline.

releases.name: Winlator bionic ref4ik mod v10
releases.body:
## Winlator Mod bionic — ref4ik v10

**What's New in This Version:**


- Icon Manager: Added support for ZIP packs and Input Bridge IPK icon packs.  (experimental)

- UI Update: The application has been fully migrated to Compose UI.

- Theme Manager: Added a new theme manager.

- Added SurfaceFlinger renderer and a choice between Vulkan/SurfaceFlinger in container settings.

- Removed wrappers v2–v6.

- Added Vulkan version selection to graphics settings.

- Added Adreno Turbo Mode (may slightly boost FPS when enabled).

- FPS Counter: Added customizable styles and color options for the FPS counter.

- Shortcut Settings: Updated the shortcut menu; navigating to the edit menu now opens a Steam game info page featuring screenshots, videos, ratings, etc.

- Updated the Steam interface and fixed various download bugs. Fixed Steam online status. Accelerated game downloads and increased the download queue limit. Added Steam Friends List with chat support. Added Steam Achievements viewer. Added library view modes: Grid, List, and Compact. Improved cloud saves. Fixed the "last played game" display in Steam.

- Sped up resource extraction on the first app launch.

- Removed several unused components, reducing the overall app size.

- Updated various system libraries.

- Integrated the MMKV library.

- Fixed CPU available extensions display.

- Improved Box/Fex component downloading inside container settings.

- Fixed a bug where enabling any screen effect caused the screen to split into two windows.

- Added an option to download drivers to the Adreno Tools store from StevenMXZ/Adreno-Tools-Drivers.

- Added GPU name selection + CustomName in CPU settings.

- Added Leegao wrapper.

- Added on-screen controls import from Input Bridge.  (experimental)

- Added selection and export of community configurations (experimental)

- Localization: Added Portuguese translation.

****Thanks to these projects — some features were taken from and implemented based on their code:****

- **Winlator Bionic** — https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator

- **Bannerlator** — https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator
- **WinNative** — https://github.com/WinNative-Emu/WinNative
- **GameNative** — https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative
- **Winlator Ludashi** — https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi



<details>
<summary>← Click here to view full previous changelog</summary>

### Previous Major Updates

🟣 Fixed FPS counter  
🟣 Fixed error in driver loading menu  
🟣 Removed limitation — now all Turnip drivers are shown in every repository (not just the first 10)  
🟣 **AdrenoTools**: added option to export downloaded driver to device storage  
🟣 Added option to hide FEX/Box config files when one is selected  
🟣 Game shortcut icons now load from **Steam**  
🟣 **Adreno Settings**: added initial parameters (work in progress)  
🟣 Integrated **AdrenoTools Store** for extended driver functionality  
🟣 Introduced **Wrapper v2** — improved driver selection UI  
🟣 Added fast **WFM** implementation from de0ver  
🟣 Added full **Russian** language support  
🟣 Reworked input controls settings:  
  → Now you can add custom icons to controls  
  → Go to control profile editor → select icons → apply  
  → Long-press icon to remove

🟣 Updated Task Manager: added GPU and battery temperature indicators  
🟣 Changed application color scheme

🟣 Fixed crash when adding Wine Proton  
🟣 **Active Windows**: now you can open/close running programs (UI/functionality to be improved later)  
🟣 Added built-in **File Manager**

- Added frame generation from If the frame generator does not work on the first container launch, try restarting the container.

- Updated FPS counter

- Added experimental Steam games launch
- Added support for animated covers for games

- Added Relative Mouse option from the container to the control settings

- Added the ability to set a custom icon for a game

- Added BCn settings from implementation Winlator Bionic 

- Improved File Explorer — now you can create a game shortcut directly from it

- Interface changes: moved menus, "Installed Components" section, and Gamepad Test

- Removed Big Picture menu from the project and cleaned up other unused code

- Created a new SystemSensorPaths class - that contains all paths to the device's system sensors and centralized all sensor queries

The Vulkan renderer implementation is based on Winlator Ludashi 

- Added new sensor paths: hwmon/MTK

- Added content provider selection in Contents (ref4ik, the412banner, and the ability to add your own custom provider)

- Added "Box/FEX Extreme" preset

- When enabling Toggle Fullscreen , the sharpness effect is now automatically enabled to improve image qualit

- Fixed translations

launch inside Wine.

</details>
