releases.name: Winlator bionic ref4ik mod v10
releases.body:
## Winlator Mod bionic — ref4ik v10

**What's New in This Version:**


- Icon Manager: Added support for ZIP packs and Input Bridge IPK icon packs.  (experimental)

- UI Update: The application has been fully migrated to Compose UI.

- Theme Manager: Added a new theme manager.

- Added SurfaceFlinger renderer and a choice between Vulkan/SurfaceFlinger in container settings.

- Removed wrappers v2–v6.

- Added Vulkan version selection to graphics settings.

- Added Adreno Turbo Mode (may slightly boost FPS when enabled).

- FPS Counter: Added customizable styles and color options for the FPS counter.

- Shortcut Settings: Updated the shortcut menu; navigating to the edit menu now opens a Steam game info page featuring screenshots, videos, ratings, etc.

- Updated the Steam interface and fixed various download bugs. Fixed Steam online status. Accelerated game downloads and increased the download queue limit. Added Steam Friends List with chat support. Added Steam Achievements viewer. Added library view modes: Grid, List, and Compact. Improved cloud saves. Fixed the "last played game" display in Steam.

- Sped up resource extraction on the first app launch.

- Removed several unused components, reducing the overall app size.

- Updated various system libraries.

- Integrated the MMKV library.

- Fixed CPU available extensions display.

- Improved Box/Fex component downloading inside container settings.

- Fixed a bug where enabling any screen effect caused the screen to split into two windows.

- Added an option to download drivers to the Adreno Tools store from StevenMXZ/Adreno-Tools-Drivers.

- Added GPU name selection + CustomName in CPU settings.

- Added Leegao wrapper.

- Added on-screen controls import from Input Bridge.  (experimental)

- Added selection and export of community configurations (experimental)

- Localization: Added Portuguese translation.

****Thanks to these projects — some features were taken from and implemented based on their code:****

- **Winlator Bionic** — https://github.com/Pipetto-crypto/winlator

- **Bannerlator** — https://github.com/The412Banner/Bannerlator
- **WinNative** — https://github.com/WinNative-Emu/WinNative
- **GameNative** — https://github.com/utkarshdalal/GameNative
- **Winlator Ludashi** — https://github.com/StevenMXZ/Winlator-Ludashi



<details>
<summary>← Click here to view full previous changelog</summary>

releases.name: Winlator bionic ref4ik mod test
releases.body: Test version for bug detection: experimental support for launching games via Steam has been added.

releases.name: Steamlator 1.7
releases.body: Split controls into two tabs: - Sensor - Gamepad In the Gamepad tab, visual gamepad button display was added. Added the **Controller Manager** button (similar to the Winlator Cmod implementation) Added support for custom icons (2 formats available: full white mode / original color mode) Added 2 new control types: - radial-menu (like in Winlator 11) - swipe-pad (allows button input using swipe gestures ← → ↑ ↓) Added visual feedback when pressing almost all button types. Added the **Quick HUD** function to container settings. It allows quick access to: - controls - keyboard Added a new **Device** tab where you can view: - CPU name - RAM usage Added **Open WineCFG** and **Open Regedit** buttons to container settings (these become available after container creation) This allows opening these programs directly inside the container in vertical mode. Now after container creation, it is possible to change the Wine / Proton version. Fixed loading of Box64 versions from Content. Added versions 0.42 and others to the **WoWBox64** tab. Added Wine versions: - 9.7 - 8.2 Added the ability to download Turnip drivers online in the **Adreno-tools** tab (from the K11MCH1 repository) Now Wine / Proton downloaded from Content will be located in: `Z:\opt` Updated the **Toggle Fullscreen** button Before: - simply stretched the canvas to 100% Now: - allows manual stretch adjustment while keeping screen borders Added a gear icon on the right side of **WineD3D**, where settings from Wine Configuration were moved. Added language switching in **Settings** New language added: - Spanish Fixed the visual freeze at 76% during ImageFS installation. Added log output during: - ImageFS installation - container startup Added clipboard copy support in the **Logs (Wine Debug)** tab. Added a new **Limit FPS** tab. Added the **export** folder inside assets which allows loading data into: `Z:\export` # Box64 / Turnip Updates Box64 0.42 Turnip 26.0 R8 / 26.0 R8 GMEM

releases.name: Steamlator 1.6
releases.body:
## Graphical Drivers – Added / Updated

- **Vortek 2.1** ([From Winlator 11](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator/releases/tag/v11.0.0))
- **Qualcomm Driver v842.6** for 8 Elite, 8 Gen4, 8eGen5 processors (from GameHub)  
- **Turnip** updated to v25.3.0 R10  
- **Turnip v25.3.0 Gmem** for xx6, xx7 series processors (Adreno 710, 720 …)  

## Other Changes

- Fixed **Proton 9.0 arm64ec** operation  
- Added **dxvk 2.7.1** to contents  
- **dxvk-2.4 gplasync** added by default  
- Buttons now **react visually when pressed** ([from Winlator coffincolors](https://github.com/coffincolors/winlator))

### ✅ Is it safe to use Steamlator?  
[Click here to check security details](https://github.com/slaker222/Steamlator/blob/winlator_bionic/security.md)

# ❓ Why use Steamlator

| # | Feature | Supported |
|---|---------|-----------|
| 1️⃣ | Access to online games | ✅ Yes |
| 2️⃣ | Download mods directly from Steam Workshop | ✅ Yes |
| 3️⃣ | Full-featured Steam client | ✅ Yes |
| 4️⃣ | Installing games from Epic Games | ✅ Yes |
| 5️⃣ | Games download to the Download folder and can be launched on any emulator | ✅ Yes |
| 6️⃣ | Save synchronization with PC | ✅ Yes |
| 7️⃣ | Conflicts with Winlator | ❌ No |

releases.name: Steamlator 1.5.2
releases.body:
🏗 Based on Winlator Just Bionic 1.5.2


🚀 Key Changes

Steam is built into the APK – when you launch the container, the Steam launcher opens automatically.
    
![photo_5792158196801063468_y](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/37e5ab07-da9e-4051-b1ba-339b44e0e50e)

Uses special launch arguments for maximum compatibility.

Verified: Steam runs on devices with Helio G99 and Snapdragon 665
(devices with more powerful processors will work as well).
    
Steamlator includes all the features of Winlator Just Bionic 1.5.2, including:

 Support for importing .wcp files

All functionality of the original Winlator Just Bionic

🖥 Windows Application Support

Steamlator uses Proton + Box64 to run Windows applications.

You can also install Wine via:
 Contents → select version 9.2 or 9.16.

🎮 DXVK Updates

Added DXVK 2.6.1 and Glansync

Added DXVK 2.7

Older DXVK versions are still available

releases.name: Winlator~Honkon v11
releases.body:
### VERSION WINLATOR V11 MOD

***Esta versión sigue siendo básica pero trae algunas mejoras en general y otras más específicas para dispositivo mediatek,gpu mali y gpu xclipse.***

### _registro_ _de_ _cambios_

•Todo los cambios de la versión oficial de Bruno.
•Se agregó el menu de contenido de la versión afeimod(creditos y agradecimiento).
•Traduje gran parte de la interfaz a español además de cambiar el logo y tema.
•Se cambió el directorio de donde se descarga el contenido,esto para agregar DXVK parcheado como stripped o sarek para mayor compatibilidad con vulkan 1.1 o 1.2 con pocas extenciones disponibles, también se agregaron varios wined3d para descargar.
•Se agregaron varías variables en la configuración de box64 para mayor rendimiento y compatibilidad.
•Se agregaron parches para el rendimiento y compatibilidad.
•se agregaron complemento necesario para distintos títulos funcionen correctamente.
•se agregaron distintas variables para rendimiento, compatibilidad y estabilidad.

releases.name: Winlator~honkon v10.1
releases.body: ### vortek+DXVK ya funciona en gpu mali Esta versión sigue siendo básica pero trae algunas mejoras en general y otras más específicas para dispositivo mediatek,gpu mali y xclipse,más adelante subiré una versión completa :) AVISO:vortek no funciona en todo los dispositivos con mediatek,esto debido a varios factores lo cuales son driver, versión de vulkan ,extenciones etc... Bruno trabajo arduamente para mejorar la compatibilidad y rendimiento de winlator en dispositivos con gpu mali y xclipse. ### Registro de cambios: 1- se uso la versión de afie mod como inspiración,créditos y agradecimiento.. Aquí la versión por si la quieren probar [mod afei](https://github.com/afeimod/winlator-mod/releases/tag/10.0-Amod5fix) 1.1• se elimino la opción de comandos y traduje la opción para agregar contenido... Tenga en cuenta que algunas cajas y controladores deben instalarse con wine9.16.whp antes de poder usarse,está opción solo es para pruebas, por favor apoya al autor original. 2•Se cambió el directorio de donde se descarga el contenido,esto para agregar DXVK parcheado como stripped o sarek para mayor compatibilidad con vulkan 1.1 o 1.2 con pocas extenciones disponibles, también se agregaron varios wined3d para descargar. 3•Se agregaron parches para el rendimiento y compatibilidad. 4•se agregaron complemento necesario para distintos títulos funcionen correctamente. 5•se agregaron distintas variables para rendimiento, compatibilidad y estabilidad.

releases.name: Winlator_Brasil_10.1, Winlator_Brasil_10.1_AI e Winlator Brasil CLT
releases.body:
### 🆕 Winlator_Brasil_10.1, Winlator_Brasil_10.1_AI e Winlator Brasil CLT – Novas Versões

Esta atualização traz duas versões, **Winlator Brasil 10.1**, **Winlator_Brasil_10.1_AI** e **Winlator Brasil CLT**. 

**Permitindo instalar as 3 versões ao mesmo tempo, no seu celular**.

![Screenshot_2025-05-28-13-46-30-893_com miui home](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/11ae82c8-c174-425a-909d-0aa1b31db7f5)


---

O **Winlator Brasil 10.1** traz diversas melhorias e recursos adicionais para aprimorar sua experiência com jogos no Android. 

O **Winlator_Brasil_10.1_AI** traz diversas melhorias e um desempenho melhor de gameplay e áudio.

O **Winlator Brasil CLT** é focado apenas em programas de trabalho do dia a dia.

--- 

Confira abaixo todas as novidades e instruções detalhadas.

---

- **NOTA:** Para utilizar o **Winlator Brasil 10.1**, você precisa desinstalar a versão original do Winlator ou Winlator Bionic ou qualquer Winlator associado ao nome do Winlator, se você já o possuía para poder instalar este mod. Ou, caso contrário, você não pode instalar este mod porque este mod é usado glibc e glibc não são suportados o nome personalizado para clonar o apk como benchmark ou qualquer outro nome.

---

### ✅ Novidades da Versão **Winlator Brasil 10.1**

- **📦 Pacote AIO Visual C++**. Instalação automática de todas as versões do Visual C++ Redistributable.
- **🌐 Novo Navegador Web**: Inclusão de um navegador leve para facilitar downloads e navegação.
- **🗜️ WinRAR**. Ferramenta de compactação/descompactação adicionada.
- **🎮 Perfis de Controles de Jogos**: Agora com 92 perfis disponíveis para download automático.

Veja como baixar os perfis de controle automaticamente:

![Download de Perfis](https://github.com/winlatorbrasil/Winlator-Brasil/blob/main/android_alsa/controle.gif )

- **📦 Instalação Offline do Wine Gecko e Mono**: Facilita a execução de aplicativos .NET e páginas com conteúdo HTML.
- **⚙️ Novas Flags do Box64**: 14 flags adicionadas para otimização e compatibilidade.
- **🌐 Novas Variáveis de Ambiente**: 17 variáveis configuráveis para ajuste fino de desempenho.
- **🇧🇷 Tradução 90% em Português (Brasil)**: Interface mais amigável para usuários brasileiros.
- **🍷 Suporte ao Wine 9.15 e 9.16 (ESTÃO EM BETA AINDA)**: Adição de menu para instalação dessas versões (Wine 10 não suportado).

`PODE ALTERNAR ENTRE AS VERSÕES DA WINE EM UM MESMO CONTAINER, SEM PRECISAR CRIAR UM NOVO CONTAINER, ISSO ESTÁ EM BETA TAMBÉM, ERROS PODEM ACONTECER.`

Aprenda a instalar as versões 9.15 e 9.16 do Wine:

![Instalação do Wine](https://github.com/winlatorbrasil/Winlator-Brasil/blob/main/android_alsa/winebug.gif )

- **🧠 Aumento da Memória Virtual**: Agora é possível configurar até 16384MB.
- **🎨 Novos Ícones nos Controles**: Facilita a edição e personalização dos controles.
- **⚡ Aumento do Limite de Taxa de Quadros**: Suporte para até 240 FPS.

---

### ✅ Novidades da Versão **Winlator_Brasil_10.1_AI**

- **⚠️ ATENÇÃO AOS DRIVERS**. Ele reconhece até:
 
**Turnip:** 24.3.0
**DXVK:** 2.5.3.0
**BOX:** 0.3.3

- **⚠️ Feito com base no Fork do foxrain versão usada foi a Winlator-9.0-Amod3_LuAi.apk**.
- **📦 Pacote AIO Visual C++**. Instalação automática de todas as versões do Visual C++ Redistributable.
- **🌐 Novo Navegador Web**: Inclusão de um navegador leve para facilitar downloads e navegação.
- **🎮 Perfis de Controles de Jogos**: Agora com 92 perfis disponíveis para download automático.

Veja como baixar os perfis de controle automaticamente:

![Download de Perfis](https://github.com/winlatorbrasil/Winlator-Brasil/blob/main/android_alsa/controle.gif )

- **🗜️ WinRAR**. Ferramenta de compactação/descompactação adicionada.
- **🇧🇷 Tradução 90% em Português (Brasil)**: Interface mais amigável para usuários brasileiros.
- **📦 Instalação Offline do Wine Gecko e Mono**: Facilita a execução de aplicativos .NET e páginas com conteúdo HTML.
- **⚡ Aumento do Limite de Taxa de Quadros**: Suporte para até 240 FPS.
- **🧠 Aumento da Memória Virtual**: Agora é possível configurar até 16384MB.

---

### ✅ Novidades da Versão **Winlator Brasil CLT**

- **⚠️ Feito com base no Fork do WB64DEV versão usada foi a Winlator-9.1.1.apk**.
- **📦 Pacote AIO Visual C++**. Instalação automática de todas as versões do Visual C++ Redistributable.
- **🌐 Novo Navegador Web**: Inclusão de um navegador leve para facilitar downloads e navegação.
- **🗜️ WinRAR**. Ferramenta de compactação/descompactação adicionada.
- **🇧🇷 Tradução 90% em Português (Brasil)**: Interface mais amigável para usuários brasileiros.
- **📦 Instalação Offline do Wine Gecko e Mono**: Facilita a execução de aplicativos .NET e páginas com conteúdo HTML.
- **💻 Instalação LibreOffice**. Instalação do pacote Office Open Source, para edição de arquivos Excel, Word e PowerPoint.
- **🖌️ Instalação Blender**: Instalação do Blender para modelagens 3D.
- **⚡ Aumento do Limite de Taxa de Quadros**: Suporte para até 240 FPS.
- **🧠 Aumento da Memória Virtual**: Agora é possível configurar até 16384MB.

### ⚙️ Explicações Técnicas 

#### 🔧 Flags do Box64 

As flags do Box64 permitem ajustar o comportamento do emulador para melhor compatibilidade e desempenho:

- `BOX64_DYNAREC_SAFEFLAGS`: Ativa flags seguras no recompilador dinâmico para evitar instruções potencialmente problemáticas.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_FASTNAN`: Otimiza operações com valores NaN (Not a Number) para maior velocidade.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_FASTROUND`: Utiliza arredondamento rápido em operações de ponto flutuante.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_X87DOUBLE`: Garante precisão dupla nas operações com o coprocessador x87.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_BIGBLOCK`: Permite blocos maiores de código no recompilador dinâmico, melhorando o desempenho.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_STRONGMEM`: Implementa verificações de memória mais rigorosas para evitar acessos inválidos.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_FORWARD`: Habilita a execução antecipada de instruções para otimização.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_CALLRET`: Otimiza chamadas e retornos de funções.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_WAIT`: Garante sincronização adequada em instruções de espera.
- `BOX64_AVX`: Ativa suporte às instruções AVX para aplicações que as utilizam.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_NATIVEFLAGS`: Utiliza flags nativas do sistema host para melhor desempenho.
- `BOX64_DYNAREC_WEAKBARRIER`: Reduz barreiras de memória para melhorar a velocidade, com possível impacto na precisão.
- `BOX64_MAXCPU`: Define o número máximo de CPUs virtuais disponíveis para o emulador.
- `BOX64_UNITYPLAYER`: Otimiza a execução de jogos desenvolvidos com o Unity Player.

#### 🌐 Variáveis de Ambiente 

As variáveis de ambiente permitem configurar aspectos específicos do sistema e do emulador:

- `LC_ALL`: Define a localidade padrão do sistema, afetando formatação de datas, números, etc.
- `ZINK_DESCRIPTORS`: Controla o número de descritores usados pelo driver Zink.
- `ZINK_DEBUG`: Ativa mensagens de depuração para o driver Zink.
- `MESA_SHADER_CACHE_DISABLE`: Desativa o cache de shaders no Mesa.
- `MESA_SHADER_CACHE_MAX_SIZE`: Define o tamanho máximo do cache de shaders.
- `MESA_GLTHREAD`: Ativa o threading no OpenGL para melhorar o desempenho.
- `WINEESYNC`: Habilita o esync no Wine para melhor gerenciamento de threads.
- `TU_DEBUG`: Ativa mensagens de depuração para o driver Turnip.
- `MESA_VK_WSI_DEBUG`: Ativa depuração para a interface de sistema de janelas no Vulkan.
- `MESA_GL_VERSION_OVERRIDE`: Força uma versão específica do OpenGL.
- `DXVK_HUD`: Exibe informações de desempenho na tela durante a execução.
- `GALLIUM_HUD`: Similar ao DXVK_HUD, mas para drivers Gallium.
- `PULSE_LATENCY_MSEC`: Define a latência do PulseAudio em milissegundos.
- `MESA_EXTENSION_MAX_YEAR`: Restringe as extensões do Mesa a um ano específico.
- `IR3_SHADER_DEBUG`: Ativa depuração para shaders no driver Freedreno.
- `WINE_DO_NOT_CREATE_DXGI_DEVICE_MANAGER`: Impede a criação do gerenciador de dispositivos DXGI no Wine.
- `MANGOHUD`: Ativa a sobreposição do MangoHud para monitoramento de desempenho.

---

### 📥 Instruções de Instalação

1. Instale o APK do **Winlator Brasil 10.1** normalmente no seu dispositivo Android.
2. Execute o aplicativo e aguarde a criação das pastas iniciais.
3. Acesse o menu de gerenciamento de pacotes para alternar ou baixar novas versões do Box64, Turnip ou DXVK.
4. Aplique os templates de controle nas configurações de mapeamento conforme o jogo desejado.

---

### 🛠️ Possíveis Bugs e Melhorias

⚠️ **Instabilidade com Wine 10**: Atualmente, o Wine 10 não é suportado devido a incompatibilidades conhecidas.  
📉 **Limitações de Desempenho**: Alguns dispositivos podem não atingir os 240 FPS devido a restrições de hardware.  
🌐 **Tradução Incompleta**: Apesar de 90% da interface estar traduzida para o português, algumas partes ainda podem aparecer em inglês.

`Obrigado: [afeimod](https://github.com/afeimod/) pelo seu belo trabalho em relação ao menu de drivers.`

Esta versão do **Winlator Brasil 10.0** (fork baseado no projeto de Bruno Dev) inclui uma ampla variedade de versões do **Box64**, **Turnip**, **DXVK** e dezenas de templates de controle otimizados para jogos populares.

---

**NOTA:** Você precisa desinstalar a versão original do Winlator ou Winlator Bionic ou qualquer Winlator associado ao nome do Winlator, se você já o possuía para poder instalar este mod. Ou, caso contrário, você não pode instalar este mod porque este mod é usado glibc e glibc não são suportados o nome personalizado para clonar o apk como benchmark ou qualquer outro nome.

---

## ✅ Componentes pré-instalados

**Box64**:  
0.3.0, 0.3.2, 0.3.3, 0.3.4, 0.3.5  

**Turnip**:  
24.1.0, 25.0.0  

**DXVK**:  
0.96, 1.7.2, 1.10.3, 2.4.1  

---

## 📥 Componentes adicionais disponíveis para instalação

**Box64**:  
0.2.7, 0.2.8, 0.2.9, 0.3.1, 0.3.5.1, 0.3.5.2, 0.3.5.3, 0.3.5.5  

**Turnip**:  
23.1.0, 23.1.6, 23.3.0, 24.0.9, 24.3.0.10, 24.3.0.5,  
25.0.0.1, 25.0.0.2, 25.0.0.3, 25.0.0.4, 25.0.0.5,  
25.1.0.2, 25.2.0.1  

**DXVK**:  
0.40.1, 0.7, 0.97, 1.4.2, 1.5.5, 1.7.1, 1.10.3.0,  
1.10.6.0, 1.10.8.0, 1.13.3, 2.2, 2.2.116, 2.3.1, 2.3.1.54,  
2.4, 2.4.24, 2.4.1, 2.4.1.1, 2.4.1.123, 2.4.1.293,  
2.5.1.2, 2.5.2, 2.5.3.0, 2.6, 2.6.1.1, 2.6.2,  
78.11, 78.9, 78to11, 78to9  

---

## 🎮 Templates de controle incluídos

RTS, Template (12 buttons), Hitman 2, Virtual Gamepad, Fallout 3, Far Cry 4,  
Risen 2, Skyrim, Deus Ex: Human Revolution, Final Fantasy Type-0, Psychonauts,  
Spider-Man: Shattered Dimensions, The Alliance Alive, Mass Effect 2, Alpha Protocol,  
Dark Souls 2, Sonic Generations, Sonic Mania, The Forest, The Saboteur, Ys Origin,  
Ultima Underworld, Bioshock, Driver Parallel Lines, Divinity 2 DC, FlatOut 2,  
Rayman 3, Turok 2, Front Mission Evolved, Gravity Circuit, Metro 2033, NFSMW,  
Marvel Ultimate Alliance, Quake 4, Turok Evolution, Unepic, Untitled Goose Game,  
Infernal, Slime Rancher, SteamWorld Dig 2, IGI 2, RAGE, Oblivion, Drakensang,  
GTA 5, La Mulana, Stalker CS, Sunset Overdrive, Xanadu Next, Cyber Shadow,  
Singularity, Wolfenstein, Prey, Shovel Knight, Second Sight,  
Call of Juarez: Gunslinger, Final Fantasy 8, Star Wars Jedi Knight 2  

---

## 🆕 Changelog

- Inclusão de múltiplas versões do Box64, Turnip e DXVK (pré-instaladas e autônomas);  
- Mais de 50 templates de controle personalizados para jogos;  
- Melhor organização para facilitar atualizações e testes de compatibilidade.

---

## 📌 Instruções de instalação

1. Instale o APK do **Winlator Brasil** normalmente no seu dispositivo Android.  
2. Execute o app e aguarde a criação das pastas iniciais.  
3. Acesse a área de gerenciamento de pacotes para alternar ou baixar novas versões de Box64, Turnip ou DXVK.  
4. Aplique os templates de controle nas configurações de mapeamento conforme o jogo desejado.  

---

## 💾 Acesso ao cartão de memória

Para acessar jogos armazenados no cartão de memória, use o app **"Gerenciador de Arquivos"** (disponível gratuitamente na Play Store).  
Nele, vá até o cartão de memória, toque e mantenha pressionada uma pasta até ela ser selecionada.  
Toque em **"Mais"** (no rodapé), vá em **"Propriedades"** e veja o campo **"Pasta"**, que exibirá o caminho completo, por exemplo:  
`/storage/9BD3-F22E/`  
Copie esse caminho e cole na seção **"Drives"** do container no Winlator.

---

## 🎮 Correção de XInput

Para corrigir possíveis bugs no joystick XInput, acesse o menu:  
**Iniciar > Fixes > XInput Fixes > XInput Fix Controller**

---

⚠️ A estabilidade e o desempenho podem variar conforme o dispositivo e a GPU utilizada.

releases.name: GameHub 5.3.5 Lite V2-hotfix
releases.body:
Changelog:
• Ported SD Card support from recent [GameHub 5.3.3 Release Candidate](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/releases/tag/v5.3.3-RC1) releases
• Merged two APKs into one - added API switch into settings, by default it's set to EmuReady API, can be switched to official GameHub API, restart the app after changing API

Hotfix:
• Included Documents Provider patch (access to /data/data without root)

releases.name: GameHub 5.3.5 Lite V1
releases.body:
Ported [GameHub Lite 5.3.3](https://github.com/Producdevity/gamehub-lite/releases/tag/v5.3.3) modification to 5.3.5, two versions included, general changes:
- Disabled Discover and Free games tabs (as they don't work in both versions)
- DocumentsProvider patch included (app's /data/ can be accessed through file managers)
- Preserved original GameHub resources (icon and stuff, lazy to replace)
- Used package name - emuready.gamehub.lite
- Signed with testkey

Official API version:
- Uses GameSir server for downloading firmware/drivers/other components, usually up to date, may have privacy concerns
- More cluttered My Games list, includes PC/PS Link (idk if it is working)
- Can import Android games
- No login required
- Offline is working

EmuReady API version:
- Uses EmuReady server to download firmware/drivers/other components, more private
- Has more GPU drivers to download, but may not have the latest components to download
- Clean My Games list (only Windows emulation and Steam)
- Can't import Android games
- No login required
- Offline is working

Only difference in APK files are https server strings, everything else is the same!

releases.name: Computers Are Totally Screwed 27
releases.body:
# Changelog

### Core
* Components list by [StevenMXZ](https://github.com/StevenMXZ) and [Xnick417x](https://github.com/Xnick417x/winlator-nightly-wcp)
* Runtime download content by [Arihany](https://github.com/Arihany/WinlatorWCPHub#-runtime-packages) and [ajay9634](https://github.com/ajay9634/Ajay-prefix)
* Store feature from [Ludashi-plus](https://github.com/The412Banner/Ludashi-plus/releases/tag/v3.1.2-pre2)
* Win components from [Winlator 11](https://github.com/brunodev85/winlator/releases/tag/v11.0.0)

### Drivers
* Add DD7to9 v1.7 wrapper
* Add new Qualcomm and Turnip drivers
* Defaults for Pico and Quest headsets updated
* DXVK framesync, MangoHUD, DXVK framerates updated
* Improvements to global default values
* Installing and updating content on app start fixed
* Set default DXVK frame limit to refresh rate

### Modding tools
* Improved game support for Reshade
* Injecting Reshade from Z drive fixed
* TrackIR emulation using opentrack_wxr integrated

### Updated UI
* Box64 RC manager moved into settings
* Close Controller Manager dialog on app pause/close
* Controller notice removed
* Controller tweaks moved from settings into Controller manager
* Default container resolutions cleanup
* Do not show Proton in contents type list
* DXVK guide into selection dialog added
* Move wheel mapping option into motion controls dialog
* Move left-handed mapping option into controller manager
* New cleaned settings UI
* Shortcut option to autoclose container added

### XrAPI v0.5
* Cache framesync mapping between the sessions
* Controller grip quaternions exposed
* Container resolution in Z:\tmp\xr\system
* HMD altitude exposed
* XServer input APIs added

### XR support
* CAS screen effect
* Controller updates mouse buttons only if state changed
* Custom keyboard instead of OS keyboard
* Gray out passthrough when in immersive mode
* Immersive mode with user defined distance
* Improve snapturn mapping
* Increase controller thumbstick deadzone
* Pixmap update frequency increased to 72Hz
* Virtual wheel mapping now uses analog joystick input

# Credits
* Drivers and wrappers were updated and tested by @GmoLargey
* The store integration has been polished by @lvonasek, @Gilleece
* TrackIR integration has been done by @bigelod

releases.name: Bringing Legacy Into Today 16
releases.body:
### 3D rendering
* Option to add/remove Reshade from shortcut dialog
* Option to force DXGI at Reshade injection
* Reshade automatic installation for DirectX 9 and higher
* Reshade popular community shaders included
* SBS support for windowed and lower resolution apps

### Bug fixes
* Crash when switching tabs fixed
* Do not load XR on unsupported devices
* Quick menu on Quest bigger
* Unsupported "Big Picture Mode" option removed

### Core
* Allow bigger screen distance
* License into the code added
* Option to support 6GB VRAM
* Select 4GB VRAM by default on container creation
* XR code refactor

### Input
* Decrease latency when typing
* Laser gun option added
* Steering wheel emulation added (experimental)

### UI
* Change tab About to More (containing online info)
* Move Adreno GPU drivers under content tab
* Quck menu rearranged
* Quick menu SBS support
* Relative mouse renamed and moved into Controller Manager
* Show controller manager in quick menu

### XR API 0.4
* Additional screen mode with head tracking
* Map haptics events to bHaptics/ProTube accessory
* Send UDP data on multiple ports

releases.name: Almost Ready to Fly 11
releases.body:
### New UI
* Automatically save container/settings
* Hide touch input controls screen
* Hide unsupported shortcut features
* Quick menu size fixed
* Remember previously opened menu screen
* Updated logo/icon
* Use tabs instead of navigation menu

### XR API 0.3
* AER support added
* Disable head->mouse mapping when UDP is running
* Expose immersive and SBS mode
* Incorrectly rounded negative values fixed
* Increase framesync cache length
* Let user control stereo
* Option to enable UDP communication without VR
* Support higher resolutions

### XR Core
* Autoclose the app when there are no windows
* CPU/GPU level and refresh rate options added
* Decrease XR latency
* Do not allow portrait container resolution
* Shortcut resolution option fixed by @bigelod
* Swapchain indexing fixed

### XR Screen features
* Bloom and fake reflection effects added
* Curved screen support
* Screen effects support in XR mode
* Screen zooming added
* Wideangle screen support

### XR Support
* [Flawless Wide Screen](https://www.flawlesswidescreen.org/)
* [OpenTrack WXR](https://github.com/bigelod/opentrackWXR)

Big thanks to @GmoLargey for testing and reporting many bugs during the development.

releases.name: Eden v0.2.1
releases.body:
## Changelog

This is a hotfix release for 0.2.0, addressing some crashes and bugs that occurred in certain situations. CPU and graphics performance will remain largely the same.

This release does *not* use SDL3, and remains on SDL2.

# Universal

- The Windows auto updater now accounts for the current build ID and will properly fetch the corresponding build to your currently installed Eden
- Fixed crashes and errors that could occur when large amounts of games and external content files were present
- Fixed crashes on 12th-gen Intel CPUs
- Fixed MK8DX freeze when the native controller applet was used
- Fixed a rare case of weird noises playing on shutdown
- Fixed mods that expect a real value out of `DefaultDataSpace`
- Fixed some minor issues with emulated controller thread contention

# Internal

- Fixed several build errors:
  - Newer Xbyak removed Xeon Phi features
  - Unused variable errors in NCE
  - Removed `httplib` version restrictions

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2 |
| Zen 4 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4 (AMD Z1/Z2, ROG Ally X, Legion Go S) |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.1-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

### Room Executables

These are statically linked Linux executables for the `eden-room` binary.

- [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/eden-room-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl)
- [x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/eden-room-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl)

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Windows-v0.2.1-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Android-v0.2.1-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Android-v0.2.1-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-Android-v0.2.1-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-macOS-v0.2.1.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.1/Eden-v0.2.1.torrent)

releases.name: Eden v0.2.0
releases.body:
## Changelog

This is the first anniversary of Eden's inception, on May 10, 2025. It has been quite a hectic journey thus far, from drama, to server nukes, to DMCAs, to sysadmin nightmares, to exotic hardware/OS ports, to constant threats from all angles. Yet still, we survive, with countless improvements, bug fixes, performance enhancements, features, and so much more to show for it.

Thank you all so, so much for the constant support in the face of continuous, unending challenges and difficulties. Here's to many more years of Eden, and for the time being, enjoy 0.2.0 as your anniversary gift!

You are encouraged to read the [detailed changelog](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/compare/v0.2.0-rc2...v0.2.0).

# Universal

- New 1st anniversary icon
- Firmware 22.0 compatibility
- Removed the separate logging thread
  - Now all logging is done in-place
- Stubbed GetFriendListForViewerV2
  - Qlaunch will no longer crash when viewing friends
- Various changes to make the finalized assembly of the binary (host executable) be cleaner and reduce indirection
  - Notably, this includes new C++ guidelines for structuring code and make compilation better
  - This doesn't affect code generated by JIT (i.e the code of the programs being ran under the emulator), only the emulator itself
- Fix Qlauncher crashing when opened twice
- Built-in auto-updater
- Common global random number generator
- Fix crashes for shared_memory
  - Fixes Tomodachi Life (boot)
- Updated firmware constants to 22.0, Atmosphere to 1.10.2
- Improve TLS fetching performance on thread_data

# Renderer

- Global maintainance Vulkan fixes and improvements for all platforms
  - Notably fixes performance and accuracy issues across the board
- Simplified texture cache GC
- Fixed index scaling for descriptors
- Mark sampled image descriptors indices as non uniform
- Handle dynamic texture descriptor strides
- Expose GPU-async for other platforms
- SPIRV-Opt has been nuked
  - This means no more SPIRV optimizations
  - ...many of which were just placebo and made no change other than making shader comp significantly slower
- Improved Lanczos filtering shader
  - Unrolling loops causes a slight improvement
- Fix CBuf get/set for composite types
- Set high Z-index to fix keyboard display
  - Mainly affects TL:LTD
- Fix Maxwell3D processing always marking dirty flags
- Vertex buffer uses static storage

# Desktop

- MSVC builds have been dropped in favour of clang-cl
  - This is mainly due to GitHub runners updating MSVC 2022 to MSVC 2026, which contains compiler bugs
- Fix mod manager bugs
- Remove fastmem ban for FreeBSD
  - Issue was with the vm_reclaim_contig in kernel when using improper flags, fixed on 15
- Fix FFmpeg not detecting VA-API on FreeBSD

# Android

- Better QCOM and Snapdragon compatibility and performance
- Added an option to control the number of Vulkan workers on Android (from 4 to 8)
- Enable legacy rescale pass on Android
  - This fixes games like LM3

# Dynarmic

- Various improvements to the dispatcher, decoder and IR constructor
  - Improves JIT latency
- Removed redundant ARM decode matcher indirection
- Implement VectorMin/Max{S,U}64 emitters
  - Makes Sonic Crossworlds, and many more games, be able to boot on macOS and WoA
- Proper MWAIT push/pop handling
  - This fixes specific CPUs who have such instruction
  - Prevents crashes
- GDB improvements
- Removed VTable bounce hack
  - This was needed for PKZA, but it's no longer necesary
- mcl dependency removed

# Internal

- Allow FFmpeg cross compilation
  - This should make porting significantly easier (OpenSSL pending)
- More documentation on debugging, compiling and such
- Fixes when using XCode CMake generator
- Fixes for RISC-V builds
  - This is not production-ready yet, but it compiles now.
- Remove shader directory regeneration
  - This breaks support with *BSD make, but fixes regeneration of the shader files
- Fix liquid glass icon on MacOS

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2, with patches for Game/Desktop mode support |
| ROG Ally X | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4, with patches for Game/Desktop mode support |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

### Room Executables

These are statically linked Linux executables for the `eden-room` binary.

- [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/eden-room-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl)
- [x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/eden-room-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl)

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

releases.name: Eden v0.2.0-rc2
releases.body:
## Changelog

This release contains a myriad of performance improvements and graphical fixes, alongside general UI and UX improvements, internal improvements, and much more. There are a **ton** of changes--far too many to go over here--so as always, go through the [full changelog](<https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/compare/v0.2.0-rc1...v0.2.0-rc2>) yourself for more details.

## Universal

- Fixed Breath of the Wild 1.9.0 not displaying the correct game name
- JIT and memory performance improvements
- VSync is now disabled if Turbo Mode is active
- Fixed audio in Pokemon Fire Red/Leaf Green

## Renderer

- Fixed invisible mice textures in Luigi's Mansion 3
- Fixed graphical issues and crashes on older Qualcomm GPUs
- Fixed various vertex explosions and color artifacting
  * This primarily affects games such as Mario Kart, Luigi's Mansion 3, and the Zelda games.
- Fixed shader bugs on newer AMD drivers on Windows
- Fixed Skyward Sword HD eye glitch

## Desktop

- Added a basic frametime and FPS overlay
  * This can be enabled with Ctrl+V
- Fixed importing of RomFS mods from zip/folder
- Fixed the game list `Title` column starting out super small by default
- Added tooltips to the game grid, showing current game version & playtime
- Added the Applets tab to the per-game configuration
- Mods can now be deleted or opened in the file manager from the per-game configuration menu
- The GPU selector box now shows its driver
- Fixed Applets tab titlebar cutoff
- Added back the IR camera (if `YUZU_USE_QT_MULTIMEDIA` is on)

## Android

- Fixed bugs and graphical oddities with game pause/resume

## Internal

- FFmpeg and SDL have been updated, which fixes a Vulkan VP9 decoding bug and adds gyro and rumble support to 8BitDo controllers respectively.
- MbedTLS has been replaced with OpenSSL. This generally improves performance, especially on custom optimized OpenSSL builds
- Added support for Polish and Thai language packs
- Fastmem and NCE are automatically disabled on Linux systems that don't support it
- Several configuration matrices have been removed:
  * `YUZU_USE_CPM`
  * `ENABLE_SDL2` (always on except for Android)
  * `ENABLE_OPENSSL` (required now)

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

**Compilers**

- **MSVC**: The default compiler for Windows. This is the most stable experience, but may lack in performance compared to any of the following alternatives.
- **GCC**: The standard GNU compiler; this is the default for Linux and will provide the most stable experience.
- **PGO**: These are built with Clang, and use PGO. PGO (profile-guided optimization) uses data from prior compilations
to determine the \"hotspots\" found within the codebase. Using these hotspots,
it can allocate more resources towards these heavily-used areas, and thus generally see improved performance to the tune of ~10-50%,
depending on the specific game, hardware, and platform. Do note that additional instabilities may occur.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | GCC | PGO | Notes |
|------------|-----|-----|-------|
| amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| 
| Legacy amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness)
| Steam Deck | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Zen 2, with additional patches for SteamOS
| ROG Ally X | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Zen 4
| aarch64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc2-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| 

### Debian/Ubuntu

Debian/Ubuntu targets are `.deb` files, which can be installed via `sudo dpkg -i <package>.deb`.

| Target | amd64 | aarch64 | Notes |
|--------|-------|---------|-------|
| Ubuntu 24.04 | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Ubuntu-24.04-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64.deb) | [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Ubuntu-24.04-v0.2.0-rc2-aarch64.deb) | Not compatible with Ubuntu 25.04 or later
| Debian 12 | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Debian-12-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64.deb) | [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Debian-12-v0.2.0-rc2-aarch64.deb) | Drivers may be old
| Debian 13 | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Debian-13-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64.deb) | [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Debian-13-v0.2.0-rc2-aarch64.deb) | 

### Room Executables

These are statically linked Linux executables for the `eden-room` binary.

- [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/eden-room-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl)
- [x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/eden-room-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl)

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.
Note that arm64 builds are experimental.

| Compiler | amd64 | arm64 | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|-------|
| MSVC | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rc2-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | 
| MinGW | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rc2-mingw-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [arm64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rc2-mingw-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | May have additional bugs/glitches
| MinGW PGO | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rc2-mingw-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | [arm64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Windows-v0.2.0-rc2-mingw-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | 

## Android

| Build  | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| [Android Standard](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-rc2-standard.apk) | Single APK for all supported Android devices (most users should use this) |
| [Android x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-rc2-chromeos.apk) | For devices running Chrome/FydeOS, AVD emulators, or certain Intel Atom Android devices. |
| [Android Optimized](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-rc2-optimized.apk) | For any Android device that has Frame Generation or any other per-device feature |
| [Android Legacy](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Android-v0.2.0-rc2-legacy.apk) | For Adreno A6xx and other older GPUs |

## macOS

macOS comes in a tarballed app. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [macOS](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-macOS-v0.2.0-rc2.tar.gz) | For Apple Silicon (M1, M2, etc)|

## Source

Contains all source code, submodules, and CPM cache at the time of release.
This can be extracted with `tar xf Eden-Source-v0.2.0-rc2.tar.zst`.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [tar.zst](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-Source-v0.2.0-rc2.tar.zst) | Source as a zstd-compressed tarball (Windows: use Git Bash or MSYS2) |
## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!),
and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.

- [Torrent](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc2/Eden-v0.2.0-rc2.torrent)

releases.name: Eden v0.2.0-rc1
releases.body:
## Changelog

This release contains several new features and UX improvements, notably:
- Updates and DLC without installing to NAND
- A grid and icon-only view for desktop
- Direct mod import from a folder or zip file
- Turbo and Slow mode toggles

And, of course, your usual dose of performance improvements, bug fixes, and regression fixes from earlier versions.
If you wish to see the *full* changelog, check out <https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/compare/v0.1.1...v0.2.0-rc1> as always.

Enjoy, and happy (late, for our more eastward friends) Valentine's Day!

## Universal

- Updates and DLCs can now be selected and used without having to install them to NAND
  * On Android, this is done in Settings -> Manage Game Folders -> \"+\" -> External Content Folder
  * On Desktop, this is done in Settings -> General -> External Content
- Added configurable speed-up and slow-down hotkeys
  * On Android, this is located in the Quick Settings menu
- Some frequently-used sets and maps have been replaced with a faster implementation
- The web token and username are now generated automatically
- The console and battery serial are now generated automatically
  * This may safeguard against adversarial mods or programs attempting to determine if they are running on an emulator.
- Improved JIT caching and page performance
- Fixed Super Mario All Stars game switching
- Fixed booting on some games that require an attached microSD card
- Fixed regressions in Sonic x Shadow and NSMBU Deluxe
- Fixed flickering and performance in Luigi's Mansion 3 (again)

## Renderer

- Improved rendering performance on Mali-based chipsets
- Improvements to macOS/MoltenVK surface handling
- Added an option to specify the target frame pacing
  * This improves frame pacing in situations where the monitor refresh rate and game FPS don't match.
- Fixed persistent ExtendedDynamicState-related graphical glitches that would occur on Windows

## Desktop

- Added a grid view
  * Game names can be turned off for a TV-like icon-only view
  * This can be accessed in the View menu
- Added the ability to import mods and cheats from a folder or zip file
- The game list now scrolls smoothly with a mouse wheel, and can be horizontally shift-scrolled as well
- Fixed Nintendo controllers having incorrect axis ordering

## Android

- Removed the now-redundant LRU Cache option
- Added a button to the home screen to launch the Switch home menu
- Added profile management, ported from the desktop implementation
- Fixed some issues with Quick Settings
- Added GPU debug logging

## Internal

- The minimum Vulkan version is now 1.4.321
- The build system is more or less stable now, and changes thereof should slow down for the foreseeable future.

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

**Compilers**

- **MSVC**: The default compiler for Windows. This is the most stable experience, but may lack in performance compared to any of the following alternatives.
- **GCC**: The standard GNU compiler; this is the default for Linux and will provide the most stable experience.
- **PGO**: These are built with Clang, and use PGO. PGO (profile-guided optimization) uses data from prior compilations
	to determine the "hotspots" found within the codebase. Using these hotspots,
	it can allocate more resources towards these heavily-used areas, and thus generally see improved performance to the tune of ~10-50%,
	depending on the specific game, hardware, and platform. Do note that additional instabilities may occur.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | GCC | PGO | Notes |
|------------|-----|-----|-------|
| amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Linux-v0.2.0-rc1-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))|

This can be extracted with `tar xf Eden-Source-v0.2.0-rc1.tar.zst`.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [tar.zst](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.2.0-rc1/Eden-Source-v0.2.0-rc1.tar.zst) | Source as a zstd-compressed tarball (Windows: use Git Bash or MSYS2) |

releases.name: Eden v0.1.1
releases.body:
# Changelog

This is a hotfix release that addresses several UI and platform-specific bugs present in the 0.1.0 release, alongside some bonus fixes and UX improvements.

- Fixed several bugs with the profile manager, such as images not being able to be set, UUIDs not matching folder names, only being able to add 4 profiles, and more
  * Alongside this, the profile manager's UX has been completely overhauled.
- Fixed some macOS-specific graphics and UI bugs
- Fixed Big Brain Academy on Linux
- Fixed blurry game images on the game list when using a scaled display on Windows
- Added back the ability to toggle Fast GPU time during gameplay
- Fixed the home menu not being usable with NCE
- Fixed the overlay applet causing crashes
- Fixed games immediately crashing on Windows 11 
- Fixed builds with OpenSSL enabled but the web service disabled

# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.

**Compilers**

- **MSVC**: The default compiler for Windows. This is the most stable experience, but may lack in performance compared to any of the following alternatives.
- **GCC**: The standard GNU compiler; this is the default for Linux and will provide the most stable experience.
- **PGO**: These are built with Clang, and use PGO. PGO (profile-guided optimization) uses data from prior compilations
	to determine the \"hotspots\" found within the codebase. Using these hotspots,
	it can allocate more resources towards these heavily-used areas, and thus generally see improved performance to the tune of ~10-50%,
	depending on the specific game, hardware, and platform. Do note that additional instabilities may occur.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | GCC | PGO | Notes |
|------------|-----|-----|-------|
| amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| 
| Legacy amd64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness)
| Steam Deck | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Zen 2, with additional patches for SteamOS
| ROG Ally X | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| Zen 4
| aarch64 | [GCC](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Linux-v0.1.1-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync))| 

### Debian/Ubuntu

Debian/Ubuntu targets are `.deb` files, which can be installed via `sudo dpkg -i <package>.deb`.

| Target | amd64 | aarch64 | Notes |
|--------|-------|---------|-------|
| Ubuntu 24.04 | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Ubuntu-24.04-v0.1.1-amd64.deb) | [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Ubuntu-24.04-v0.1.1-aarch64.deb) | Not compatible with Ubuntu 25.04 or later
| Debian 12 | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Debian-12-v0.1.1-amd64.deb) | [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Debian-12-v0.1.1-aarch64.deb) | Drivers may be old
| Debian 13 | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Debian-13-v0.1.1-amd64.deb) | [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Debian-13-v0.1.1-aarch64.deb) | 

### Room Executables

These are statically linked Linux executables for the `eden-room` binary.

- [aarch64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/eden-room-aarch64-unknown-linux-musl)
- [x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/eden-room-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl)

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.
Note that arm64 builds are experimental.

| Compiler | amd64 | arm64 | Notes |
|----------|-------|-------|-------|
| MSVC | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Windows-v0.1.1-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | 
| MinGW | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Windows-v0.1.1-mingw-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [arm64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Windows-v0.1.1-mingw-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | May have additional bugs/glitches
| MinGW PGO | [amd64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Windows-v0.1.1-mingw-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | [arm64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Windows-v0.1.1-mingw-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | 

## Android

| Build  | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| [Android Standard](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Android-v0.1.1-standard.apk) | Single APK for all supported Android devices (most users should use this) |
| [Android x86_64](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Android-v0.1.1-chromeos.apk) | For devices running Chrome/FydeOS, AVD emulators, or certain Intel Atom Android devices. |
| [Android Optimized](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Android-v0.1.1-optimized.apk) | For any Android device that has Frame Generation or any other per-device feature |
| [Android Legacy](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Android-v0.1.1-legacy.apk) | For Adreno A6xx and other older GPUs |

## macOS

macOS comes in a tarballed app. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [macOS](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-macOS-v0.1.1.tar.gz) | For Apple Silicon (M1, M2, etc)|

## Source

Contains all source code, submodules, and CPM cache at the time of release.
This can be extracted with `tar xf Eden-Source-v0.1.1.tar.zst`.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| [tar.zst](https://stable.eden-emu.dev/v0.1.1/Eden-Source-v0.1.1.tar.zst) | Source as a zstd-compressed tarball (Windows: use Git Bash or MSYS2) |

releases.body:
Nightly build of commit [`89004124a5`](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/compare/eb9280dedf..89004124a55a5ce8c45a90dcb60afd8a107238c3).

## Changelog

- [video_core] use bool params for read/writes and cascade them thru the calltree (#4001) [`89004124a5`](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/commit/89004124a55a5ce8c45a90dcb60afd8a107238c3)
  - PR #[4001](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/pulls/4001) merged on 2026-07-18 by lizzie


# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|

| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-89004124a5-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-89004124a5-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2 |
| Zen 4 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-89004124a5-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-89004124a5-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4 (AMD Z1/Z2, ROG Ally X, Legion Go S) |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-89004124a5-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-89004124a5-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Windows-89004124a5-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Windows-89004124a5-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Windows-89004124a5-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Windows-89004124a5-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Windows-89004124a5-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Windows-89004124a5-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Windows-89004124a5-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Android-89004124a5-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Android-89004124a5-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-Android-89004124a5-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-macOS-89004124a5.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784487248.89004124a5/Eden-89004124a5.torrent)

releases.name: Eden Nightly - Jul 14 2026
releases.body:
Nightly build of commit [`8b8034a2a0`](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/compare/9a0e6b3c28..8b8034a2a004d160d1dea6b229d53c16cf2558d3).

## Changelog

- [dist, android] Update translations from Transifex for Jul 14 (#4205) [`8b8034a2a0`](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/commit/8b8034a2a004d160d1dea6b229d53c16cf2558d3)
  - PR #[4205](https://git.eden-emu.dev/eden-emu/eden/pulls/4205) merged on 2026-07-14 by Eden CI


# Packages

## Targets

Each build is optimized for a specific architecture and uses a specific compiler.

- **aarch64/arm64**: For devices that use the armv8-a instruction set; e.g. Snapdragon X, all Android devices, and Apple Silicon Macs.
- **amd64**: For devices that use the amd64 (aka x86_64) instruction set; this is exclusively used by Intel and AMD CPUs and is only found on desktops.
- **v3**: For devices that use the x86_64-v3 instruction set or newer; this is found on Ryzen, Intel 4th Generation (Haswell), and newer.

### PGO

PGO builds usually perform ~10-30% better than standard builds, and are thus generally recommended for all users.

## Linux

Linux packages are distributed via AppImage.
[zsync](https://zsync.moria.org.uk/) files are provided for easier updating, such as via
[AM](https://github.com/ivan-hc/AM).

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-----------|-------|
| amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-amd64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-amd64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |
| Legacy amd64 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-legacy-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-legacy-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Pre-Ryzen or Haswell CPUs (expect sadness) |
| Steam Deck | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-steamdeck-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 2 |
| Zen 4 | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-rog-ally-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) | Zen 4 (AMD Z1/Z2, ROG Ally X, Legion Go S) |
| ARM (aarch64) | [Standard AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-aarch64-gcc-standard.AppImage.zsync)) | [PGO AppImage](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-8b8034a2a0-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage) ([zsync](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Linux-aarch64-clang-pgo.AppImage.zsync)) |  |

## Windows

Windows packages are in-place zip files. Setup files are soon to come.

| Build Type | Standard | PGO (Recommended) | Notes |
|------------|----------|-------------------|-------|
| amd64/x86_64 (MSVC) | [MSVC zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Windows-8b8034a2a0-amd64-msvc-standard.zip) |  | Use if you encounter graphical issues on other builds (e.g. Pokemon Scarlet & Violet) |
| amd64/x86_64 v3 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Windows-8b8034a2a0-amd64-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Windows-8b8034a2a0-amd64-clang-pgo.zip) | Built with MinGW. Requires Ryzen, 4th gen Intel, or newer |
| Zen 4 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Windows-8b8034a2a0-rog-ally-gcc-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Windows-8b8034a2a0-rog-ally-clang-pgo.zip) | Requires Zen 4 or newer (e.g. ROG Ally X, Legion Go S). Incompatible with Intel |
| aarch64/arm64 | [Standard zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Windows-8b8034a2a0-arm64-clang-standard.zip) | [PGO zip](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Windows-8b8034a2a0-arm64-clang-pgo.zip) | Snapdragon devices |

## Android

| Build  | Notes |
|--------|-------|
| [Standard APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Android-8b8034a2a0-standard.apk) | The standard build. Most users should use this. |
| [Genshin Spoof APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Android-8b8034a2a0-optimized.apk) | Spoofs Eden as Genshin Impact, which may enable optimizations/frame generation on some flagship devices. |
| [Legacy APK](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-Android-8b8034a2a0-legacy.apk) | For Snapdragon 865 and other unsupported chipsets |

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-macOS-8b8034a2a0.dmg)

## Torrent

A torrent containing all artifacts. To use this, simply download the torrent and import it into your
favorite torrent client (BitTorrent only!), and artifacts will be downloaded automatically.
- [Torrent](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1784055937.8b8034a2a0/Eden-8b8034a2a0.torrent)

## macOS

macOS comes in a DMG image. These builds are currently experimental, and you should expect major graphical glitches and crashes.
In order to run the app, you *may* need to go to System Settings -> Privacy & Security -> Security -> Allow untrusted app.

- [macOS DMG](https://nightly.eden-emu.dev/v1783882429.defb8bf2e2/Eden-macOS-defb8bf2e2.dmg)
